zh: 艺术不是你看到的，而是你让别人看到的。
en: Art is not what you see, but what you make others see.

zh: 所有艺术家必须一半是诗人，一半是工匠。
en: All artists must be half poet, half craftsman.

zh: 艺术的目的是洗去我们灵魂上日常生活的尘埃。
en: The purpose of art is washing the dust of daily life off our souls.

zh: 最好的艺术家知道该省略什么。
en: The best artists know what to leave out.

zh: 《星夜》绘制于1889年6月，当时文森特·梵高正住在一家精神病院里。
en: The Starry Night was painted in June 1889 when Vincent van Gogh was staying at a home for the mentally ill.

zh: 梵高画下了他从窗户看到的景象——布满云朵、星星和月亮的夜空。
en: Van Gogh painted what he saw from his window — the night sky with clouds, stars and a moon.

zh: 然而，他不仅仅画了云朵。
en: However, he didn't just paint the clouds.

zh: 他还画了巨大的白色和黄色圆圈在天空中疾驰。
en: He also painted the massive circles of white and yellow racing across the sky.

zh: 星星和月亮明亮地悬挂在夜空中。
en: The stars and the moon hang bright in the night sky.

zh: 在这片令人惊叹的天空下，我们看到了一个沉睡的村庄和一棵黑暗、孤独的树。
en: Beneath this amazing sky, we see a sleeping village and a dark, lonely tree.

zh: 他对色彩不寻常的运用使得专家们认为，梵高的精神疾病可能影响了他的视觉。
en: His unusual use of colour has led experts to think that Van Gogh's mental illness may have affected his sense of sight.

zh: 他还服用了一种会让人看到黄色斑点的药物，就像《星夜》中的星星一样。
en: He also took a drug that can make people see yellow spots, just like the stars in The Starry Night.

zh: 不幸的是，梵高独特的画作并不受欢迎，他在有生之年只卖出了一幅画。
en: Unfortunately, Van Gogh's unique paintings weren't very popular and he only sold one painting in his lifetime.

zh: 他也认为《星夜》是一幅失败的作品。
en: He also thought The Starry Night was a failure.

zh: 然而，事实是，《星夜》现在是世界上最著名的画作之一。
en: However, the fact remains that The Starry Night is now one of the world's most famous paintings.

zh: 《呐喊》由爱德华·蒙克于1893年绘制。
en: The Scream was painted by Edvard Munch in 1893.

zh: 它引人注目的原因是它展示了一个瘦弱的身影，表情恐惧——身影的嘴张得很大，发出强有力的尖叫。
en: What makes it striking is that it shows a thin figure with an expression of fear — the figure's mouth is wide open and letting out a powerful scream.

zh: 这个身影捂着他或她的耳朵，直视着观看者。
en: The figure covers his or her ears and looks directly at the viewer.

zh: 这个身影被安置在一条桥上，桥下是黑暗、暴风雨中的大海，背景是燃烧般的橙红色天空。
en: This figure is set on a bridge above a dark, stormy sea, and against a burning orange-red sky.

zh: 蒙克在他的日记中谈到了是什么启发了他。
en: In his diary, Munch talked about what inspired him.

zh: “太阳下山时，我和两个朋友正在路上走着；突然，天空变得像血一样红……我站在那里颤抖。
en: “I was walking down the road with two friends when the sun set; suddenly the sky turned as red as blood ... I stood there shaking.

zh: 然后我听到了大自然的尖叫。”
en: Then I heard the scream of nature.”

zh: 许多专家说，《呐喊》与蒙克的心理健康问题有关，这给他带来了很多痛苦。
en: Many experts say that The Scream is connected to Munch's mental health problems, which caused him a lot of pain.

zh: 《光明帝国》是比利时艺术家勒内·马格利特在1953年至1954年间创作的一系列画作之一。
en: The Empire of Light is among a series of paintings by Belgian artist René Magritte between 1953 and 1954.

zh: 他将同一场景画了27次。
en: He painted the same scene 27 times.

zh: 它展示了一座美丽的房子，内部灯光通明，被夜晚的黑暗所包围。
en: It shows a beautiful house lit by lights from inside, surrounded by the darkness of night.

zh: 奇怪的是，在房子和树的上方，我们看到一个白天的天空，充满了明亮和柔和的白云。
en: What is strange is that above the house and the tree, we see a daytime sky full of brightness and soft white clouds.

zh: 这似乎是一个相当神秘的场景。
en: It seems like quite a mysterious scene.

zh: 马格利特是一位受其思想和观念启发的画家。
en: Magritte was a painter inspired by his thoughts and ideas.

zh: 他的画作是视觉实验，因为他试图玩弄现实。
en: His paintings were visual experiments as he tried to play with reality.

zh: 他的观点是，艺术应该震撼观众并挑战他们的现实感。
en: His view was that art should shock the viewers and challenge their sense of reality.

zh: 《光明帝国》在观众中产生了不同的反应。
en: The Empire of Light has produced different reactions in viewers.

zh: 有些人觉得这些画看起来黑暗且令人不安。
en: Some feel that the paintings look dark and troubling.

zh: 然而，另一些人则觉得它们令人平静。
en: Others, however, find them calming.

zh: 至于马格利特本人，他认为画中昼夜的变化是令人惊讶的。
en: As for Magritte himself, he thought that the change between day and night in the paintings was surprising.

zh: “我称这种力量为：诗意，”他说。
en: “I call this power: poetry,” he said.

zh: 我很高兴你喜欢它。
en: I'm so glad you enjoyed it.

zh: 我很担心因为……
en: I was worried because ...

zh: 我喜爱舞蹈和杂技。
en: I loved the dancing and the acrobatics.

zh: 表演者们太有才华了！
en: The performers were so talented!

zh: 但有很多事情我不明白。
en: But there were quite a few things I didn't understand.

zh: 我希望我对它们了解得更多。
en: I wish I knew more about them.

zh: 众所周知，路德维希·范·贝多芬是一位音乐天才，但可能很少有人知道他是如何创作《D小调第九交响曲》以及它的首演是如何进行的。
en: Everyone knows that Ludwig van Beethoven is a musical genius but few might know how he created Symphony No. 9 in D minor and how its first show went.

zh: 路德维希·范·贝多芬是一位德国作曲家。
en: Ludwig van Beethoven was a German composer.

zh: 他被认为是音乐史上最伟大的作曲家之一。
en: He is regarded as one of the greatest composers in the history of music.

zh: 在他二十多岁时，他因其钢琴技巧而闻名，但随后他开始失去听力。
en: In his twenties, he had been very famous for his skills on the piano, but then he began to lose his hearing.

zh: 贝多芬曾想过放弃，但最终他继续创作音乐。
en: Beethoven thought about giving up, but in the end he continued to write music.

zh: 受其与耳聋斗争的启发，这位作曲家创作出了一些惊人的作品，包括九部交响曲、五首钢琴曲和一部歌剧！
en: Inspired by his struggles with deafness, the composer produced some amazing pieces, including nine symphonies, five piano pieces, and an opera!

zh: 他持续作曲直至1827年去世，创作了130多部音乐作品，包括他的《D小调第九交响曲》。
en: He proceeded with the composition until his death in 1827, writing more than 130 musical works, including his Symphony No. 9 in D minor.

zh: 1824年2月的一天，在奥地利维也纳的小房子里，路德维希·范·贝多芬靠在椅子上，笑了。
en: One day in February 1824, at his little house in Vienna, Austria, Ludwig van Beethoven sat back in his chair and smiled.

zh: 这位著名德国作曲家的第九交响曲终于完成了。
en: The famous German composer's ninth symphony was finally completed.

zh: 创作这部作品花了好几年时间，而现在最后的音符已被添加到乐谱上。
en: Writing the piece had taken several years, and now the final notes had been added to the score.

zh: 54岁的他并不知道这将是他最后一部交响曲。
en: At 54 years of age, he didn’t know that this would be his last symphony.

zh: 当他在页面底部自豪地签上自己的名字时，贝多芬试图想象人们第一次听到它时会作何反应。
en: As he proudly signed his name at the bottom of the page, Beethoven tried to imagine how people would respond when they heard it for the first time.

zh: 演出前，维也纳一家著名剧院的后台气氛十分紧张。
en: Before the performance, the backstage atmosphere at a very famous theatre in Vienna was tense.

zh: 贝多芬担心演出会是一场灾难。
en: Beethoven was afraid that the performance would be a disaster.

zh: 毕竟，一个听不见自己乐团的指挥有什么用——即使他是个音乐天才？
en: After all, what use is a conductor who could not hear his orchestra — even if he is a musical genius?

zh: 当这位著名作曲家12年来首次走上舞台时，观众毫不犹豫地大声鼓掌。
en: The audience did not hesitate to applaud loudly as the famous composer walked out onto the stage for the first time in 12 years.

zh: 剧院的音乐总监迈克尔·乌姆劳夫加入了他，两人共同负责乐团。
en: The theatre's musical director, Michael Umlauf, joined him and together the two men took charge of the orchestra.

zh: 一个多小时里，贝多芬在乐团前跳来跳去，在空中疯狂地挥舞着手臂，并疯狂地翻着乐谱。
en: For more than an hour, Beethoven jumped about in front of the orchestra, waving his arms wildly in the air, and madly turning the pages of his score.

zh: 整个过程中，乌姆劳夫静静地站在他身边，熟练地引导乐团演奏完这首世界上已知的最令人惊叹的乐曲。
en: The whole time, Umlauf stood quietly by his side, skilfully guiding the orchestra through the most amazing piece of music the world had ever known.

zh: 当最后一个欢乐的音符标志着交响乐的结束时，观众们跳了起来，鼓掌、欢呼并挥舞着帽子。
en: As the final, joyous note signalled the end of the symphony, the audience jumped to their feet, clapping, cheering and waving their hats.

zh: 但贝多芬继续指挥，他的头埋在乐谱里。
en: But Beethoven continued conducting, his head buried in the score.

zh: 直到其中一位歌手卡罗琳·昂格尔拉住他的胳膊，让他转身面对观众，这位伟人才意识到他的交响乐取得了成功。
en: It was not until Caroline Unger, one of the singers, took his arm and turned him to face the audience that the great man realised his symphony was a success.

zh: 后来，卡罗琳回忆说，贝多芬并不是唯一一个感到惊讶的人。
en: Later, Caroline remembered that Beethoven was not the only one who got a surprise.

zh: “观众们也震惊了，”她微笑着说。
en: “The audience was shocked as well,” she said with a broad smile.

zh: “他们中的大多数人不知道他聋了！
en: “Most of them had no idea that he was deaf!

zh: 房间里唯一没有听到这首交响乐——也永远不会听到——的人正是创作它的人。”
en: The one person in the room who didnt hear the symphony —and never would — was the very man who composed it.”

zh: 我写信是想咨询关于参加你们电影节的信息。
en: I am writing to ask for information about entering a film in your festival.

zh: 我十六岁，是第一中学的学生。
en: I am sixteen years old and I am a student at No. 1 Senior Secondary School.

zh: 我三年前开始制作音乐视频，并且非常喜欢它。
en: I started making music videos three years ago, and I am very fond of it.

zh: 去年，我制作了我的第一部短片。
en: Last year, I made my first short film.

zh: 我想询问关于你们电影节的影片。
en: I would like to ask about the films for your festival.

zh: 你们接受体育纪录片吗？
en: Do you accept sport documentaries?

zh: 我目前正在和一位同学合作制作一部体育纪录片。
en: I am currently working on a sport documentary with a school friend.

zh: 在过去的几个月里，我们一直在采访和拍摄年轻球员关于他们对篮球的兴趣。
en: For the past few months, we have been interviewing and filming young players about their interest in basketball.

zh: 您能告诉我这种类型的影片是否可以接受吗？
en: Could you tell me if this kind of film would be acceptable?

zh: 我想知道您是否也能给我更多关于如何申请参加这个电影节的信息。
en: I wonder if you could also give me more information about how to apply to be part of this festival.

zh: 团体和个人都可以参加吗？
en: Can groups as well as individuals enter?

zh: 我们可以同时将我们的影片提交到另一个电影节吗？
en: Can we enter our film into another film festival at the same time?

zh: 此外，您能告诉我关于奖项的情况吗？
en: In addition, would it be possible to tell me about the prizes?

zh: 是否有针对不同年龄组和不同类型影片的奖项？
en: Are there prizes for different age groups and for different types of films?

zh: 感谢您的帮助，期待您的回复。
en: I appreciate your help and look forward to hearing from you.

zh: 张大千，1899年出生于四川省，是中国最杰出、最受欢迎的艺术家之一。
en: Zhang Daqian, born in 1899 in Sichuan Province, was one of China's most brilliant and popular artists.

zh: 他不仅极其多产——据说他平均每年创作500幅画作——而且还能掌握多种绘画风格，从细致的肖像画到富有表现力的风景画。
en: Not only was he extremely productive — it is said that he created an average of 500 paintings a year — but he was also able to master a variety of painting styles, ranging from detailed portraits to expressive landscapes.

zh: 张大千出生于一个艺术世家，最初由他的母亲和兄弟姐妹教他绘画。
en: Born into an artistic family, Zhang Daqian was first taught how to paint by his mother and siblings.

zh: 他还广泛旅行，磨练技艺：从在日本学习着色技巧到在敦煌研究佛教壁画。
en: He also travelled extensively, honing his craft: from learning colouring techniques in Japan to studying Buddhist mural paintings in Dunhuang.

zh: 然而，中国传统绘画一直是他生活中的一大热情所在，他总是认为自己属于那个传统。
en: However, traditional Chinese painting remained one of the great passions of his life, and he always considered himself part of that tradition.

zh: 巴勃罗·毕加索是20世纪上半叶最具影响力的欧洲艺术家之一。
en: Pablo Picasso was one of the most influential European artists of the first half of the 20th century.

zh: 他开创了立体主义，也影响了象征主义和超现实主义。
en: He pioneered Cubism and also influenced Symbolism and Surrealism.

zh: 他是一位画家和雕塑家，游走于多种不同的风格之间。
en: He was a painter and a sculptor who moved between many diverse styles.

zh: 毕加索于1881年出生在西班牙马拉加市的一个富有创造力的家庭。
en: Picasso was born in 1881, into a creative family in the Spanish city of Malaga.

zh: 他的父亲是一位画家，他鼓励并教导年轻的巴勃罗。
en: His father was a painter and he encouraged and taught young Pablo.

zh: 毕加索的母亲 famously claimed that his first word was “pencil”。
en: Picasso’s mother famously claimed that his first word was “pencil”.

zh: 当他年轻的时候，他的家人会去马德里观看西班牙古代大师的作品。
en: When he was young, his family would visit Madrid to see works by the old Spanish masters.

zh: 1956年，张大千在法国尼斯的家中拜访了毕加索。
en: In 1956, Zhang Daqian visited Picasso at his home in Nice, France.

zh: 据说这两位艺术家互相钦佩。
en: It is said that the two artists admired each other.

zh: 在那次会面中，他们互相赠送了一些艺术品。
en: At that meeting, they gave each other some artworks.

zh: 毕加索总共给了张大千600件作品，全都是他模仿中国画的画作。
en: Picasso gave Zhang 600 works in total, all of which were his imitations of Chinese paintings.

zh: 他请张大千教他如何画竹子。
en: He asked Zhang to teach him how to draw bamboo.

zh: 这次会面之后，两位艺术家成为了朋友，并在张大千回到中国后互通书信。
en: Following this meeting, the two artists became friends and exchanged letters after Zhang's return to China.

zh: 这两位艺术家的共同点不仅仅是相互钦佩。
en: The two artists had more in common than mutual admiration.

zh: 两人在风格上都极其多样化，备受钦佩，影响力巨大且非常高产。
en: Both were incredibly diverse in their styles, much admired, influential and very prolific.

zh: 2011年，张大千将他的朋友毕加索从世界最畅销艺术家的榜首位置推了下来。
en: In 2011, Zhang Daqian pushed his friend Picasso off the top of the list of best-selling artists in the world.

zh: 涂鸦是写在公共场合墙上的文字、图画或标记。
en: Graffiti is writings, drawings or marks made on walls in public places.

zh: 在大多数情况下，它是用颜料或喷漆创作的。
en: In most cases, it is created with paint or spray paint.

zh: 有时它只是一个人的名字或一个粗鲁的词语。
en: Sometimes it is just a person’s name or a rude word.

zh: 有时它被视为一种公共抗议。
en: Sometimes it is regarded as a public protest.

zh: 如今，涂鸦通常是文字和图画的混合体，通常用一个标签签名，这是一个艺术家或团体独有的签名。
en: Today, graffiti is often a mixture of writing and pictures, usually signed with a tag, which is a signature unique to an artist or group.

zh: 涂鸦如今在全世界都很流行，甚至艺术博物馆和画廊都在收藏街头艺术家的作品。
en: Graffiti is now popular all over the world, and even art museums and galleries are collecting the works of street artists.

zh: 然而，关于涂鸦存在很多争议。
en: However, there are many controversies over graffiti.

zh: 一些人认为未经业主许可进行的涂鸦是犯罪，而另一些人则将其视为一种丰富的非传统文化表达形式。
en: Some think it is a crime if done without a property owner's permission, while others see this as a rich form of non-traditional cultural expression.

zh: 据信，现代形式的涂鸦首次流行是在20世纪60年代的美国。
en: It is believed that graffiti in its modern form first became popular in the United States in the 1960s.

zh: 像纽约这样城市的年轻人会用特制罐子里的颜料在城市周围的墙上喷涂他们的标签。
en: Young adults in cities like New York would use paint in special cans to spray their tag on walls around the city.

zh: 这个标签是他们创造的一个名字，用以标识他们自己和他们的艺术作品。
en: This tag was a name they created to identify themselves and their artwork.

zh: 一些涂鸦壁画和绘画是标记城市帮派或非法犯罪集团地盘的标志。
en: Some graffiti murals and paintings were signs marking the territories of city gangs or illegal crime groups.

zh: 纽约人过去常常将贫困社区和地铁列车墙上的涂鸦视为一种威胁，并视其为城市衰败的一个例子。
en: New Yorkers used to see the graffiti on the walls of poor neighbourhoods and subway trains as something threatening and as an example of urban decay.

zh: 这些杂乱的名字和口号被视为丑陋和粗糙，是破坏者寻求自我表达甚至提出政治观点的工作。
en: The messy names and slogans were seen as ugly and rough, the work of vandals seeking to express themselves or even to make a political point.

zh: 直到20世纪70年代，大多数人都讨厌涂鸦，认为它令人厌恶。
en: Up to the 1970s, most people hated graffiti, considering it disgusting.

zh: 它是非法的，并会受到罚款的惩罚。
en: It was illegal and punishable by fines.

zh: 后来，涂鸦和涂鸦艺术家的形象发生了很大变化，涂鸦不再只出现在地铁和城市的贫困地区。
en: Later, the image of graffiti and graffiti artists changed a lot and graffiti was no longer only found in the subways and the poor areas of cities.

zh: 在20世纪80年代初，出现了一股对涂鸦艺术甚至曼哈顿艺术世界的狂热。
en: In the early 1980s, there was a craze for graffiti art and even the Manhattan art world.

zh: 如今，涂鸦具有'街头艺术'的地位，并已成为一个独立的运动，表达居住在大城市中的年轻人的街头文化。
en: Nowadays, graffiti has the status of 'street art' and has become a separate movement expressing the street culture of young people living in big cities.

zh: 你可以在你最意想不到的地方找到涂鸦——在广告中、在衣服上、在玩具上，甚至在报纸和其他媒体中。
en: You can find graffiti in places where you'd least expect it — in advertisements, on clothes, on toys, and even in newspapers and other media.

zh: 大型国际公司现在意识到了涂鸦在广告中的吸引力。
en: Large international companies now realise the appeal of graffiti in advertising.

zh: 凯尔·罗德里格斯，曾经为纽约地铁列车作画，现在担任美国最大报纸之一的艺术总监。
en: Kel Rodriguez, who used to paint New York subway trains, now works as an art director for one of the biggest newspapers in the United States.

zh: 他还为许多其他报纸和杂志工作。
en: He also works for a number of other newspapers and magazines.

zh: 伦纳德·麦格尔，一位有25年经验的街头艺术家，从为地铁列车作画转向为年轻人设计和销售受涂鸦启发的服装。
en: Leonard McGurr, a street artist for 25 years, went from painting subway trains to designing and marketing graffiti-inspired clothes for young people.

zh: '涂鸦一直是一个关于生存的故事，'他说。
en: 'Graffiti has been a story of survival,' he says.

zh: '有一种方法可以从你的工作中获益，而不会破坏公共财产。'
en: 'There’s a way to benefit from your work without spoiling public property.'

zh: 齐白石（1864-1957）是中国最伟大的画家之一。
en: Qi Baishi (1864-1957) was one of China's greatest painters.

zh: 在1902年至1909年间，他游历了中国各地，并画了许多风景画。
en: Between 1902 and 1909, he travelled across China and painted many pictures of scenery.

zh: 他后来的兴趣转向了描绘日常生活的简单画作，例如蔬菜、花卉、鸟类和昆虫。
en: His interest later changed to simple pictures of everyday life, such as vegetables, flowers, birds and insects.

zh: 大白菜是他作品中一个著名的主题。
en: Chinese cabbages are a well-known subject of his works.

zh: 在他的一幅作品中，大白菜旁边有一只小昆虫。
en: In one of his works, there is a tiny insect near the Chinese cabbage.

zh: 它使这幅画变得特别。
en: It makes the painting special.

zh: 它的背上有些红色的斑纹。
en: It has some red markings on its back.

zh: 它黑色的眼睛紧盯着大白菜，显示出这个小生物对蔬菜的兴趣。
en: Its black eyes, which are fixed on the Chinese cabbage, show the creature's interest in the vegetable.

zh: 注意到齐白石的绘画风格是很有趣的。
en: It is interesting to note Qi Baishi's style of painting.

zh: 他的绘画风格常常让观众猜测作品的含义，并让他们发挥想象力。
en: His style of painting often leaves the audience guessing as to the meaning of the work and makes them use their imagination.

zh: 他的画作受到高度重视。
en: His paintings are highly valued.

zh: 他的绘画风格就是其原因。
en: His style of painting is the reason for this.

zh: 你觉得昨天的京剧怎么样？
en: How did you like the Beijing Opera yesterday?

zh: 我在网上读过关于京剧的介绍。
en: I have read about the Beijing Opera online.

zh: 终于能在舞台上看到它了。
en: to finally see it on the stage.

zh: 戏服色彩缤纷，唱腔也很棒。
en: The costumes were colourful and the singing was great.

zh: 你没能理解故事，因为有时语言非常令人困惑。
en: that you couldn't understand the story because at times the language was very confusing.

zh: 我没能理解整个故事。
en: that I couldn't understand the whole story.

zh: 也许我需要多读一些关于这部歌剧的资料来更好地理解它。
en: Perhaps I need to read more about the opera to understand it better.

zh: 这听起来是个好主意。
en: That sounds like a good idea.

zh: 那我们以后可以多聊聊这个故事。
en: Then we can talk more about the story later.

zh: 受到滋养的生物不会互相伤害；平行的道路不会互相干扰。
en: Living things that are nourished will not injure one another; roads that run parallel will not interfere with one another.

zh: 如果我们破坏了环境，我们将不会拥有社会。
en: We won't have a society if we destroy the environment.

zh: 气候变化是一个可怕的问题，它绝对需要被解决。
en: Climate change is a terrible problem, and it absolutely needs to be solved.

zh: 它理应成为一个巨大的优先事项。
en: It deserves to be a huge priority.

zh: 我们使用资源的方式仿佛我们有两个星球，而不是一个。
en: We are using resources as if we had two planets, not one.

zh: 不可能有'B计划'，因为没有'B星球'。
en: There can be no 'Plan B' because there is no 'Planet B'.

zh: 大多数人患有一种被称为'唯我主义'的毛病。
en: Most people suffer from what is known as "Just-me-ism".

zh: 比如说，你刷牙时让水龙头一直开着，出门时让灯亮着，或者你丢了一片垃圾却懒得捡起来。
en: Well, say you leave the tap running while you brush your teeth, leave a light on when you go out or you drop a piece of litter and can’t be bothered to pick it up.

zh: 你知道所有这些事都是不对的，但那又怎样？
en: You know that all those things are wrong, but so what?

zh: 你对自己说：'这又能有什么影响呢？'
en: "What difference can it possibly make?" you say to yourself.

zh: '世界上有数百万人，而我仅仅是我自己。
en: "There are millions of people in the world and I’m just me.

zh: 我让一个小小的水龙头开着，一盏小小的灯亮着，或者在路上留下一小片垃圾，这又有什么关系呢？
en: How can it matter if I leave one little tap running, one little light on or leave a little piece of litter on the road?

zh: 而且反正也没人会知道。'
en: And anyway, no one will know."

zh: 当然，如果世界上只有少数几个人，这确实无关紧要。
en: Of course, it wouldn't matter if there were just a few people in the world.

zh: 但是，试想一下，数百万的人都对自己说：'这没关系。
en: But just think of all the millions of people saying to themselves: "It doesn’t matter.

zh: 而且，反正没人知道。'
en: And, anyway, no one knows."

zh: 数百万加仑的水会被浪费掉；数百万盏灯会一直亮着；数百万片垃圾会被丢弃。
en: Millions of gallons of water would be wasted; millions of lights would be left on; millions of pieces of litter would be dropped.

zh: 而所有这一切都将是非常有害的。
en: And all of this would be very harmful.

zh: 为了激励年轻人为环境、动物和他们的社区采取行动，根与芽组织成立了。
en: To inspire young people to take action for the environment, animals and their community, Roots & Shoots was established.

zh: 该机构由简·古道尔博士于20世纪90年代初建立，她是一位科学家，是世界上最著名的野生黑猩猩专家。
en: This institute was set up in the early 1990s by Dr. Jane Goodall, a scientist who's the world’s best-known expert on wild chimpanzees.

zh: 除了她的研究，她还全心全意地致力于环境保护。
en: In addition to her research, she has been whole-heartedly committed to environmental protection.

zh: 根与芽的全部目的是教育年轻人，从学龄前儿童到大学生，这样他们就能帮助建立一个安全的未来，并与自然和谐共处。
en: The whole purpose of Roots & Shoots is to educate young people, from pre-schoolers to university students, so they can help to build a future that is secure and live together in peace with nature.

zh: 该组织被称为根与芽，因为根在地下缓慢移动以奠定坚实的基础，而芽看起来小而弱，但它们可以破开砖墙以获取阳光。
en: The organisation is called Roots & Shoots because roots move slowly under the ground to make a firm foundation, and shoots seem small and weak, but they can break open brick walls to reach the light.

zh: 根和芽就是你、你的朋友和世界各地的年轻人。
en: The roots and shoots are you, your friends and young people all around the world.

zh: 成千上万的根与芽能够解决问题，改变世界，使其成为一个更美好的居住地。
en: Hundreds and thousands of roots and shoots can solve the problems, change the world and make it a better place to live in.

zh: 当你让一个悲伤的人微笑，让一只狗摇尾巴，给一株口渴的植物浇水时，你认为你是在帮助让世界变得更美好吗？
en: Do you think you help to make the world a better place when you make a sad person smile, when you make a dog wag its tail, and when you water a thirsty plant?

zh: 一个振作起来的人，一只快乐的狗，一株开花的植物，还有你。
en: One cheered-up person, one happy dog, one flowering plant, and you.

zh: 这就是根与芽的全部意义：每个个体——无论是人、动物还是植物——的价值和重要性。
en: That’s what Roots & Shoots is all about: the value and importance of each individual — human, animal or plant.

zh: 简·古道尔博士认为，最重要的一点是：'每一个个体都很重要。
en: Dr. Jane Goodall believes that the most important thing is that: "Every individual matters.

zh: 每一个个体都扮演着一个角色。
en: Every individual has a role to play.

zh: 每一个个体都能带来改变。'
en: Every individual makes a difference."

zh: 你可以选择：你愿意用你的一生为人类、动物和环境创造一个更美好的世界吗？
en: You get to choose: Do you want to use your life to make the world a better place for people, animals and the environment?

zh: 你愿意让根与芽在每个人心中扎根吗？
en: Do you want to make Roots & Shoots rooted in everyone's heart?

zh: 你愿意成为共建共享未来社区的一份子吗？
en: Do you want to be a part of a community for a shared future?

zh: 正是通过以这种令人兴奋的方式共同行动，我们才能让成千上万——实际上是数百万——的人参与进来。
en: It is by acting together, in this exciting way, that we can involve thousands — indeed millions — of people.

zh: 这样我们才能让世界变得更美好。
en: That’s how we can make the world a better place.

zh: 阿姆斯特丹被称为“自行车之城”。
en: Amsterdam is called "the City of Bicycles".

zh: 阿姆斯特丹的人们多年来一直享受着骑自行车的好处。
en: People in Amsterdam have been enjoying the benefits of cycling for years.

zh: 这个想法大约始于50年前，最初被称为“白色自行车”。
en: The idea began about 50 years ago, and was first known as "white bikes".

zh: 阿姆斯特丹是一个适合骑自行车的城市，因为它地势平坦，因此对自行车很方便。
en: Amsterdam is a good city for cycling because it’s flat and therefore convenient for bikes.

zh: 这里也有大量的自行车停放处，而且市中心的大多数街道都有自行车道。
en: There are also plenty of places for bicycle parking and most streets in the city centre have a bicycle path.

zh: 由于其对自行车的便利性，阿姆斯特丹深受骑自行车者的欢迎——惊人的38%的出行都是骑自行车完成的。
en: Because of its convenience for bicycles, Amsterdam has become very popular with cyclists — an amazing 38% of all trips are made by bike.

zh: 有些人甚至称阿姆斯特丹为“自行车之城”。
en: Some people even call Amsterdam "the City of Bicycles".

zh: “白色自行车”的想法是从哪里来的？
en: Where did the idea of "white bikes" come from?

zh: 在20世纪60年代，一群自行车爱好者想出了一个主意。
en: In the 1960s, a group of cycling fans came up with an idea.

zh: 他们认为，如果市中心不允许汽车通行而只允许自行车通行，对每个人都会更好。
en: They believed that it would be better for everybody if cars weren't allowed in the city centre and only bicycles were.

zh: 他们希望这有助于节约能源、减少污染并提供免费的公共交通。
en: They were hopeful that this would help to save energy, reduce pollution and provide free public transport.

zh: 该组织将数百辆自行车漆成白色，并将它们放置在阿姆斯特丹周围的许多区域供人们使用。
en: The group painted hundreds of bicycles white and placed them in many areas around Amsterdam for people to use.

zh: 任何人都可以取用它们，并用于短途出行。
en: Anyone was allowed to take them and use them for short journeys.

zh: 人们会在结束旅程的地方留下自行车，以便其他人可以使用它。
en: People would leave the bike in the place where they finished their journey, so that someone else could use it.

zh: 20 他们旅程结束的地方，这样其他人就可以从那里取车并使用它。
en: 20 place where they finished their journey, so that someone else could then take it and use it from there.

zh: 然而不久之后，问题出现了，“白色自行车”全部消失了——小偷在几周内就把它们全部偷走了！
en: Soon after, however, problems came up and the “white bikes” all disappeared — thieves stole them all in a matter of weeks!

zh: 1999年，“白色自行车”重返阿姆斯特丹——这次配备了一个计算机跟踪系统来记录它们的每一次移动！
en: In 1999, the “white bikes” returned to Amsterdam — this time with a computer tracking system to record their every move!

zh: 要取一辆自行车，你必须插入一张特殊的卡。
en: To take a bicycle, you had to put in a special card.

zh: 新的“白色自行车”实际上并不是白色的，而是被漆成了鲜艳的颜色。
en: The new “white bikes” were not actually white but painted in bright colours.

zh: 这些自行车停放在特殊的停车点。
en: The bikes were parked at special parking places.

zh: 因此，想使用它们的人必须将它们带到目的地附近的另一个特殊停车点，并把车留在那里。
en: Thus people who wanted to use them had to take them to another special parking place near their destination and leave them there.

zh: 多亏了许多人的想法和努力，比如20世纪60年代的自行车爱好者以及那些促成“白色自行车”回归的人，你现在可以在阿姆斯特丹市中心享受清洁的空气和便捷的交通。
en: Thanks to the ideas and efforts of many people, like the cycling fans of the 1960s and those who enabled the return of “white bikes”, you can now enjoy clean air and easy transport in central Amsterdam.

zh: 如今，“白色自行车”的理念已经骑行到世界各地，全球范围内的共享单车数量有所增加。
en: Nowadays, the idea of “white bikes” has pedalled its way around the world and there has been a global increase in bike-sharing.

zh: 在中国，越来越多的城市拥有自己的“白色自行车”项目。
en: In China, more and more cities have their own “white bike” programmes.

zh: 当地人和游客都喜欢使用共享单车，因为共享单车是一种廉价且简单的方式，可以节约能源，减少空气和噪音污染，并在城市中享受锻炼的好处。
en: Both the locals and tourists like to use shared bikes because bike-sharing is a cheap and easy way to save energy, reduce air and noise pollution, and enjoy the benefits of exercise in cities.

zh: 然而，问题也可能出现，比如自行车被盗和停车问题。
en: However, problems can also develop, like the theft of bikes and parking issues.

zh: 幸运的是，人们正在尝试用不同的方法来解决这些问题。
en: Fortunately, people are trying different ways of solving those problems.

zh: 共享单车在中国将何去何从？你来决定。
en: Where will bike-sharing go in China? You decide.

zh: 去年八月，市政府官员采访了约500名居民，以了解人们对保护环境的态度以及他们采取了哪些行动。
en: Last August, city officials interviewed about 500 residents to find out people’s attitudes to protecting the environment and what actions they had taken.

zh: 如图所示，认为需要帮助保护环境的人比例高达96%。
en: As the diagram shows, the percentage of those who felt that they needed to help protect the environment is as high as 96%.

zh: 极少数人（4%）认为没有必要。
en: Very few (4%) felt it was not necessary.

zh: 超过一半的受访者在过去一个月里完成了五项活动中的四项。
en: More than half of those who took part in the survey had done four of the five activities in the past month.

zh: 约90%的人回收了纸张、金属、塑料或玻璃；超过三分之二（70%）的人尝试减少使用一次性包装，如塑料蜂蜜瓶、黄油和酸奶容器。
en: Around 90% had recycled paper, metal, plastic or glass; and more than two thirds (70%) had tried to cut back on the use of disposable packaging, such as plastic honey bottles, and butter and yoghurt containers.

zh: 此外，大多数居民骑自行车或步行。
en: Also, the majority of residents had cycled or walked.

zh: 不幸的是，参与调查的人中，没有那么多人在家中采取节水节能行动。
en: Unfortunately, not as many who took part had taken action to save water and energy in their homes.

zh: 略低于40%（38%）的人尝试在洗碗或淋浴时节约用水。
en: Just under 40% (38%) tried to save water when they were washing dishes or taking showers.

zh: 略超过一半（51%）的人尝试通过减少使用空调或关灯来节约能源。
en: Just over half (51%) tried to save energy by reducing the use of air conditioning or turning off lights.

zh: 总之，似乎几乎所有参与者都理解需要帮助照顾环境。
en: To sum up, it seems that almost all who took part understood the need to help take care of the environment.

zh: 他们中的大多数人尝试回收废物、骑自行车或步行，并减少使用一次性包装。
en: Most of them tried to recycle waste, ride bikes or walk, and reduce the use of disposable packaging.

zh: 然而，没有那么多人在节约用水和能源。
en: However, not as many were saving water and energy.

zh: 这表明居民需要更加关注这两项活动。
en: This indicates that the residents needed to pay more attention to these two activities.

zh: 废物管理是一个主要的全球性问题。
en: Waste management is a major global issue.

zh: 在欧洲，瑞典在回收利用方面树立了一个很好的榜样。
en: In Europe, Sweden is setting a great example when it comes to recycling.

zh: 尽管该国每年产生约450万吨家庭垃圾，但其中只有不到1%最终进入垃圾填埋场。
en: Even though the nation produces around four and a half million tonnes of household waste each year, less than 1% of that waste ends up in landfills.

zh: 那么它去了哪里？
en: So where does it go?

zh: 嗯，很多垃圾被焚烧——该国大约一半的家庭和工业垃圾被转化为能源。
en: Well, a lot of waste is burnt — around half of the country’s household and industrial waste is turned into energy.

zh: 瑞典有32个清洁燃烧的垃圾发电厂，通过焚烧垃圾来发电。
en: Sweden has 32 clean-burning waste-to-energy plants, which burn waste to produce electricity.

zh: 根据瑞典政府的说法，三吨垃圾所含的能量相当于一吨燃料油。
en: According to the Swedish government, three tonnes of waste contains as much energy as one tonne of fuel oil.

zh: 不能焚烧的废物——废金属、塑料、玻璃和食物残渣——被重复利用或回收。
en: Waste that can't be burnt — scrap metal, plastic, glass and food scraps — is reused or recycled.

zh: 瑞典的垃圾发电厂能够焚烧的垃圾比该国产生的还要多。
en: Sweden's waste-to-energy plants are able to burn more waste than the country produces.

zh: 因此，瑞典政府现在从其他欧洲国家进口垃圾，如英国、挪威、意大利和爱尔兰。
en: As a result, the Swedish government now imports waste from other European countries, like the UK, Norway, Italy and Ireland.

zh: 在这种情况下，每个人都是赢家——对瑞典来说是一笔好生意，而欧洲其他地区的垃圾填埋场垃圾也更少了。
en: Everybody wins in this situation — it is good business for Sweden and less waste for landfills in the rest of Europe.

zh: 根据世界银行的数据，全世界每年产生约20亿公吨的垃圾。
en: According to the World Bank, the world produces around 2 billion metric tonnes of waste each year.

zh: 如果废物处理不当，可能导致疾病、污染和其他环境问题。
en: If the waste is not disposed of properly, it can lead to disease, pollution and other environmental issues.

zh: 尽管这是一个大问题，但许多地方社区正在提出处理自己垃圾的解决方案。
en: Although this is a big problem, many local communities are coming up with solutions to deal with their own waste.

zh: 在整个非洲大陆，这些人正在处理这个问题。
en: All across the African continent, these individuals are tackling the problem.

zh: 在南非，一家公司用回收塑料制作书包；在埃塞俄比亚，旧汽车轮胎和衣服被制成鞋类，销往世界各地。
en: In South Africa, a company makes schoolbags from recycled plastic, and in Ethiopia, old car tyres and clothing are turned into footwear that’s sold around the world.

zh: 这些企业正在防止废物最终进入垃圾填埋场。
en: These businesses are preventing waste from ending up in landfills.

zh: 在大多数人看到问题的地方，一些聪明人却看到了机会。
en: Where most people see a problem, a few clever individuals have seen opportunities.

zh: 为他人树立了一个如何充分利用糟糕情况的绝佳榜样。
en: Setting a great example for others about how to make the best of a bad situation.

zh: 在许多方面，过去几十年里生活变得舒适和便利得多。
en: In many ways, life has become far more comfortable and convenient in the past few decades.

zh: 然而，这是以环境为巨大代价的。
en: However, this has come at a huge cost to the environment.

zh: 在此，我们关注当前面临的三个最严重的环境问题。
en: Here, we look at three of the most serious environmental problems we are currently facing.

zh: 空气污染的主要原因是车辆和发电厂燃烧化石燃料。
en: The main cause of air pollution is the burning of fossil fuels by vehicles and power plants.

zh: 其他人类活动，如建筑和焚烧垃圾，也加剧了空气污染。
en: Other human activities, such as construction and the burning of waste, also contribute to air pollution.

zh: 这对我们健康的影响可能非常严重。
en: The effects on our health can be very serious.

zh: 根据世界卫生组织的数据，每年有240万人死于与空气污染直接相关的原因。
en: According to the World Health Organisation, 2.4 million people die annually from causes directly related to air pollution.

zh: 幼儿、老人以及有心脏和肺部问题的人因此环境问题而受害最深。
en: Young children, the elderly and people with heart and lung problems suffer the most as a result of this environmental problem.

zh: 你可以通过仅在绝对必要时才使用汽车出行，来轻松地为减少空气污染做出贡献。
en: You can easily contribute to reducing air pollution by only using a car to make journeys when it's absolutely necessary.

zh: 如果不是，尝试改用公共交通工具。
en: If it isn’t, try using public transport instead.

zh: 全球变暖是地球近地表空气和海洋温度的升高。
en: Global warming is the increase in the temperature of the Earth’s near-surface air and oceans.

zh: 它是由大气中二氧化碳和其他污染物的积聚引起的。
en: It's caused by the build-up of carbon dioxide and other pollutants in the atmosphere.

zh: 这些物质捕获太阳的热量并使地球变暖。
en: These trap the Sun's heat and warm up the planet.

zh: 在过去100年里，全球平均气温上升了0.5°C到1°C。
en: Over the past 100 years, average temperatures around the world have risen by between 0.5°C and 1°C.

zh: 科学家预测，在21世纪期间，气温将进一步上升1°C至3.5°C。
en: Scientists predict that temperatures will rise by a further 1°C to 3.5°C during the 21st century.

zh: 全球变暖已经导致了广泛的问题。
en: Global warming has already caused a wide range of problems.

zh: 由于极地冰盖融化，海平面正在上升。
en: Sea levels are rising due to the melting of polar ice sheets.

zh: 世界各国都面临着极端天气条件，如飓风、洪水和热浪。
en: Countries around the world have faced extreme weather conditions, such as hurricanes, floods and heat waves.

zh: 例如，2016年11月，法国南部和意大利北部经历了极端暴雨和时速1000公里的狂风。
en: For example, the south of France and northern Italy experienced extremely heavy rain and 1,000km/h winds in November 2016.

zh: 仅一夜之间降雨量就超过了200毫米。
en: Over 200mm of rain fell in just one night.

zh: 这导致了意大利都灵附近的波河泛滥。
en: This resulted in the flooding of the River Po near Turin in Italy.

zh: 世界上许多国家使用垃圾填埋场进行废物处理。
en: Landfills are used for waste disposal in many countries around the world.

zh: 然而，由于我们生活方式产生的废物量，这些垃圾填埋场现在正达到满负荷。
en: However, due to the amount of waste our lifestyles produce, these landfills are now reaching full capacity.

zh: 当这种情况发生时，废物中的危险化学品进入我们供水的可能性就会增加。
en: When this happens, the chances of dangerous chemicals in the waste entering our water supply increase.

zh: 在过去的十年里，中国垃圾填埋场的废物量呈指数级增长。
en: The amount of waste in landfills in China has risen exponentially over the last decade.

zh: 例如，在2005年，上海的垃圾填埋场含有略超过620万吨的废物，但现在它们含有790万吨——增长了近30%。
en: In 2005, for instance, landfills in Shanghai contained a little over 6.2 million tonnes of waste, but they now contain 7.9 million tonnes — an increase of nearly 30%.

zh: 在香港，预计现有的三个垃圾填埋场将在未来十年内填满。
en: In Hong Kong, it is predicted that the three existing landfills will be full within the next decade.

zh: 政府现在正试图寻找替代的废物处理方法。
en: The government is now trying to find alternative waste disposal methods.

zh: 一些城市，如北京、深圳和上海等，正引领潮流，并承诺到2020年实现“零废物填埋”。
en: Some cities, such as Beijing, Shenzhen and Shanghai, among others, are leading the way and have promised to send “zero waste to landfill” by 2020.

zh: 除了三个R（减少、再利用、再循环）之外，一些专家已经开始倡导第四个R——责任。
en: Apart from the three Rs (Reduce, Reuse and Recycle), some experts have begun advocating the fourth R — Responsibility.

zh: 他们认为，尽管回收废物是有效的，但解决垃圾填埋场溢出问题的最有效方法是鼓励人们承担起减少自己产生的废物的责任。
en: They believe that, although recycling waste is effective, the most effective way of solving the problem of overflowing landfills is to encourage people to take responsibility for reducing the waste they produce themselves.

zh: 简·古道尔致力于保护野生动物已有50多年，是世界上最著名的野生黑猩猩专家。
en: Jane Goodall has worked to protect wildlife for over 50 years and is the world’s best-known expert on wild chimpanzees.

zh: 除了她的研究之外，她还为绿色生活做出了巨大贡献。
en: In addition to her research, she also contributes greatly to green living.

zh: 她曾说过，许多人患有一种被称为‘唯我主义’的问题。
en: She has said that many people suffer from what is known as “Just-me-ism”.

zh: 在20世纪90年代初，简·古道尔创立了‘根与芽’组织。
en: In the early 1990s, Jane Goodall established Roots & Shoots.

zh: 这是一个促进环境保护并赋予年轻人为环境、动物和他们的社区采取积极行动的组织。
en: It's an organisation that promotes environmental protection and empowers young people to take positive action for the environment, animals and their community.

zh: 易解放是一位具有巨大力量的女性，人们非常钦佩她的工作。
en: Yi Jiefang is a woman of great strength and people admire her work very much.

zh: 她的生活在一场车祸夺去她儿子生命后发生了改变。
en: Her life changed after a car accident took the life of her son.

zh: 在他去世之前，她的儿子关心沙尘暴和内蒙古沙漠日益扩大的问题。
en: Before he died, her son was concerned about the sandstorms and the increasing size of the deserts in Inner Mongolia.

zh: 为了纪念她的儿子，她创立了一个名为‘NPO绿色生命’的慈善组织。
en: To remember her son, she founded a charity organisation called NPO-Greenlife.

zh: 许多人被她的故事感动，并成为该组织的志愿者。
en: Many people were moved by her story and became volunteers for the organisation.

zh: 该组织在中国北部地区植树。
en: The organisation plants trees in the northern region of China.

zh: 树木的存活率现在是85%，这使得该组织的工作非常有效。
en: The survival rate of the trees is 85% now, which makes the organisation's work very effective.

zh: 陈阳为她的班级项目采访了一位环保主义者刘教授。
en: Chen Yang held an interview with an environmentalist, Prof. Liu, for her class project.

zh: 谢谢您，刘教授。您提到了许多关于我们可以做些什么来帮助环境的有趣想法。
en: Thank you, Prof. Liu. You have mentioned many interesting ideas about what we can do to help our environment.

zh: 当然。城市中的共享单车有很大的好处，因为它有助于减少道路上的汽车数量，从而减少污染并带来更清洁的空气。
en: Certainly. Bike-sharing in cities has huge benefits because it helps cut down the number of cars on the road, which reduces pollution and leads to cleaner air.

zh: 当然。它还能省钱，因为用户不必为此支付太多费用。
en: Sure. It also saves money as users don't have to pay much for it.

zh: 我不得不承认，由于这是一个新想法，存在一些问题。
en: I have to admit that as it is a new idea, there are some problems.

zh: 一些城市规划者说共享单车系统太昂贵，我们应该专注于更便宜的公交车之类的事情。
en: Some city planners have said that the bike-sharing system is too expensive and we should focus on things like cheaper buses.

zh: 我理解他们所说的。
en: I understand what they are saying.

zh: 例如，在一些已经实施共享单车系统的城市，有些人故意损坏自行车，这增加了成本。
en: For example, in some cities where bike-sharing systems are in place, some people have damaged the bikes on purpose, which increases costs.

zh: 但我认为所有当前的问题都可以解决。
en: But I think all the current issues can be solved.

zh: 我们只需要耐心一点，因为它们目前还不完美。
en: We just need to be patient as they are still not perfect at the moment.

zh: 陈曦为他的项目在关心汽车使用的学生中做了一项调查。
en: Chen Xi did a survey for his project among students who are concerned about the use of cars.

zh: 路上开车的人太多了。
en: There are too many people driving on the road.

zh: 大多数人使用汽车时没有考虑使用的必要性，许多汽车只有司机一个人使用，没有任何乘客。
en: Most people use cars without thinking about the necessity of using them, and many cars are used by only a driver without any passengers.

zh: 看到燃料被浪费是令人沮丧的。
en: It's frustrating to see fuel wasted.

zh: 汽车使用的增加使空气污染成为一个非常严重的问题。
en: The increasing use of cars makes air pollution a very serious problem.

zh: 人类、动物和植物生活在污染的空气中有害。
en: It is harmful for humans, animals and plants to live with polluted air.

zh: 汽车也会造成噪音污染。
en: Cars also cause noise pollution.

zh: 根据研究，汽车是城市环境中噪音污染的主要来源。
en: According to research, cars are the main source of noise pollution in urban environments.

zh: 我住在一条主干道附近，我发现一些司机大声按汽车喇叭制造噪音，甚至在晚上也是如此。
en: I live near a main road and I find some drivers make a noise by beeping their car hooters loudly, even at night.

zh: 我们需要减少汽车的使用，并使公共交通便宜且高效。
en: We need to reduce the use of cars and make public transport cheap and efficient.

zh: 当堵在路上时，我总是感到烦恼。
en: When stopped in a traffic jam, I always feel annoyed.

zh: 昨天早上，我爸爸开车送我去学校花了一个小时，因此他上班迟到了。
en: Yesterday morning, it took my dad an hour to drive me to school in his car and therefore he was late for work.

zh: 你知道汽车在堵车时停着比行驶时产生更多的空气污染吗？
en: Do you know that cars produce more air pollution while stopped in a traffic jam than when they are moving?

zh: 学而不思则罔。
en: He who learns but does not think is lost.

zh: 思而不学则殆。
en: He who thinks but does not learn is in great danger.

zh: 无知与其说是一种耻辱，不如说是不愿学习。
en: Being ignorant is not so much a shame, as being unwilling to learn.

zh: 我相信生命最美好的意义和我们经历中最幸福的结果，就是学习。
en: I believe that the most beautiful meaning of life and the happiest result of our experiences, is learning.

zh: 教育永无止境。
en: There is no end to education.

zh: 并不是说你读了一本书，通过了一次考试，就完成了教育。
en: It is not that you read a book, pass an examination, and finish with education.

zh: 整个生命，从你出生到你死去的那一刻，都是一个学习的过程。
en: The whole of life, from the moment you are born to the moment you die, is a process of learning.

zh: 大多数人认为人类大脑是“自动”设置的——这意味着它完全靠自己学习。
en: Most people assume that the human brain is set on “automatic” — that means it learns all by itself.

zh: 但这并不总是正确的。
en: But this isn't always true.

zh: 我们需要训练自己成为更好的学习者——积极参与学习过程并反思我们所学到的东西。
en: We need to train ourselves to be better learners — to actively take part in the learning process and to reflect on what we have learnt.

zh: 这类学习行为被称为“主动学习”。
en: These kinds of learning behaviours are called “active learning”.

zh: 我建议做五件事来在你的学习中扮演主动角色。
en: I suggest doing five things to take an active role in your learning.

zh: 有两种声音：内在声音和外在声音。
en: There are two kinds of voices: the inner voice and the outer voice.

zh: 你的内在声音表达你的个人观点，而外在声音则告诉你从你所听到或读到的东西中得出的观点。
en: Your inner voice expresses your personal opinions, while the outer voice tells you about opinions from what you hear or read.

zh: 虽然你的内在声音可能有用，但它也可能妨碍学习。
en: Although your inner voice can be useful, it can also get in the way of learning.

zh: 相反，积极的学习者持开放态度，专注于说话者/作者所说的内容，而不是他们大脑在后台所说的内容。
en: Instead, active learners are open-minded and focus on what the speaker / writer is saying, not on what their brain is saying in the background.

zh: 这样，他们就能更好地做出决策。
en: In this way, they are in a better position to make decisions.

zh: 如果你发现自己的内心声音难以控制，你可以像大多数积极的学习者那样与它争论。
en: If you find your inner voice difficult to control, you can argue with it as most active learners do.

zh: 当你的内心声音告诉你说话者/作者是错的时，想想为什么说话者/作者可能是对的。
en: When your inner voice tells you a speaker / writer is wrong, think about why the speaker / writer may be right.

zh: 灵活对待你的观点，你最终可能会同意说话者/作者的观点。
en: Be flexible in your opinions and you might end up agreeing with the speaker / writer after all.

zh: 如果没有，至少你‘听到了’另一种观点。
en: If not, at least you have “listened to” another point of view.

zh: 提问是促进积极学习的最简单方法。
en: Asking questions is the easiest way to promote active learning.

zh: 当你从某人、书籍或互联网获取信息时，就这个话题问两个、三个，甚至五个问题。
en: When you get information from someone, from books or the Internet, ask two, three, even five questions about the topic.

zh: 答案将引导你进行更深层次的学习，而构思问题的行为本身将帮助你达到对主题更高层次的理解。
en: The answers will lead you to further learning, and the very act of working out questions will help you to achieve a higher level of understanding about the topic.

zh: 简而言之：不要停止好奇。
en: In short: Do not stop being curious.

zh: 积极的学习者不会接受他们学到的一切。
en: Active learners do not accept everything they learn.

zh: 他们试图在每个想法的核心找到真相。
en: They attempt to find the truth at the heart of each idea.

zh: 即使一个想法听起来完全不可能，它也可能有某个方面是基于事实的。
en: Even when an idea sounds entirely unlikely, there may be an aspect of it that is based on truth.

zh: 所以，如果有人今天说恐龙仍然存在，想想他们为什么相信这一点。
en: So if someone says that dinosaurs still exist today, think about why they believe this.

zh: 这个想法从何而来？
en: Where does the idea come from?

zh: 他们有足够的数据来支持他们的观点吗？
en: Do they have enough data to support their views?

zh: 如果你试图找出一个想法的来源，无论它看起来多么疯狂，你都会增加学到东西的机会。
en: If you try to find out the source of an idea, no matter how crazy it seems, you will increase your chance of learning something.

zh: 许多人错过了学习机会，因为他们让情绪妨碍了自己。
en: Many people miss out on learning opportunities because they let their feelings get in the way.

zh: 他们因为说话者/作者的身份而拒绝学习或忽视所说的话。
en: They refuse to learn or ignore what is said because of who the speaker / writer is.

zh: 确实，我们难免会不喜欢一些人——毕竟，这是人性。
en: It is true that we cannot help disliking some people — this is human nature, after all.

zh: 但不要忘记你仍然可以向他们学习。
en: But do not forget you can still learn from them.

zh: 积极的学习者不会根据第一印象或个人感觉来判断人。
en: Active learners do not judge people based on first impressions or personal feelings.

zh: 相反，他们将信息与传递者分开。
en: Instead, they separate the message from the messenger.

zh: 反之亦然——不要仅仅因为某些人的身份或仅仅因为他们是你的朋友就认为他们总是对的。
en: This goes the other way as well — do not just assume that some people are always right because of who they are or just because they are your friends.

zh: 我们可以做很多事情来成为更好的学习者。
en: There are many things we can do to become better learners.

zh: 我们都有两种声音。
en: We all have two voices.

zh: 我们的内在声音告诉我们个人观点，而我们的外在声音处理他人的观点和我们听到的事情。
en: Our inner voice tells us personal opinions and our outer voice deals with others’ opinions and things we have heard.

zh: 如果我们过多地倾听内心的声音，它可能会阻碍我们学习。
en: If we listen to our inner voice too much, it can block us from learning.

zh: 如果你的内心声音难以控制，你可以不同意它。
en: If your inner voice is difficult to control, you can disagree with it.

zh: 促进主动学习的最佳方法之一是提问。
en: One of the best ways to promote active learning is to ask questions.

zh: 这有助于你获得关于某个主题的更多知识。
en: This helps you gain more knowledge about a topic.

zh: 总之，我们永远不应停止好奇。
en: In a word, we should never stop being curious.

zh: 主动学习者还试图质疑他们周围的世界，并理解每个想法深处的真相。
en: Active learners also try to question the world around them and understand the truth deep within every idea.

zh: 最后，作为主动学习者，我们永远不应基于第一印象形成对人的看法。
en: Finally, as active learners, we should never form opinions about people on the basis of first impressions.

zh: 记忆专家杰迈玛·格里亚兹诺夫回答了一些关于记忆的最常见问题。
en: Memory expert Jemima Gryaznov answers some of the most common questions about memory.

zh: 为什么我能记得童年的事件，却不记得上周发生了什么？
en: Why can I remember events in my childhood but not what happened last week?

zh: 我们记得那些在我们脑海中有强烈联系的事物，尤其是情感联系。
en: We remember things that have strong connections in our mind, especially emotional connections.

zh: 童年记忆通常非常情感化。
en: Childhood memories are often very emotional.

zh: 这是因为当我们第一次经历事物时，我们常常有强烈的恐惧或兴奋感。
en: This is because when we experience things for the first time, we often have strong feelings of fear or excitement.

zh: 此外，我们童年中有趣或搞笑的故事经常被反复讲述。
en: Also, interesting or funny stories from our childhood are often told again and again.

zh: 结果，我们更好地记住了它们，因为复述事件有助于将经历固定在我们的记忆中。
en: As a result, we remember them much better, as retelling events helps fix experiences in our memories.

zh: 有些人真的拥有过目不忘的记忆力吗？
en: Do some people really have a photographic memory?

zh: 拥有过目不忘记忆力的人可以在多年后记住一张图片、一本书或一个事件的每一个细节，但没有人证明真的有人拥有过目不忘的记忆力。
en: A person with a photographic memory could remember every detail of a picture, a book or an event many years later, but no one has proved that there are people who really have photographic memories.

zh: 然而，确实有一些人拥有惊人的记忆力。
en: Yet, there are some people who do have amazing memories.

zh: 例如，丹尼尔·塔米特能记住圆周率π的前22,514位数字，而斯蒂芬·威尔特希尔在乘坐直升机飞越一座城市后，能凭记忆画出该城市的详细图画。
en: For example, Daniel Tammet can remember the first 22,514 digits of pi (π) and Stephen Wiltshire can draw a detailed picture of a city from memory after flying over it in a helicopter.

zh: 他们都擅长在有限的时间内记住特定的事物。
en: They are both good at remembering particular things for a limited time.

zh: 为什么我会忘记昨天学的新单词？
en: Why do I forget the new words that I learnt yesterday?

zh: 对许多人来说，这很自然。
en: This is natural for many people.

zh: 1885年，赫尔曼·艾宾浩斯出版了一本名为《记忆》的书，并提出了著名的遗忘曲线。
en: In 1885, Hermann Ebbinghaus published a book called Memory and presented a famous forgetting curve.

zh: 根据他的说法，最严重的记忆丧失发生在学习后的最初阶段。
en: According to him, the sharpest loss of memory occurs during the very early period after learning.

zh: 这意味着在此期间及时复习，并多次回顾所学内容，可以显著帮助我们记住信息。
en: This means timely review during this period, with a few revisits to what is learnt, can significantly help us to remember the information.

zh: 我16岁，但有时会忘记事情。
en: I'm 16, but I sometimes forget things.

zh: 我的记忆力在变差吗？
en: Is my memory getting worse?

zh: 我们的记忆力在25岁时达到顶峰。
en: Our memory reaches its full power at the age of 25.

zh: 那时，我们一秒钟能记住多达200条信息。
en: At that point, we can remember up to 200 pieces of information in a second.

zh: 然而，在这个年龄之后，大脑开始变小。
en: After this age, however, the brain starts to get smaller.

zh: 到40岁时，我们每天会失去10,000个脑细胞。
en: By the age of 40, we lose 10,000 brain cells every day.

zh: 到中年时，我们的记忆力明显比年轻时差。
en: By middle age, our memory is significantly worse than when we were young.

zh: 我们能从这一切中学到什么？当记住新事物时，尝试将其与我们的情感联系起来。
en: What can we learn from all this? When remembering something new, try to connect it to our emotions.

zh: 将其与我们已知的事物联系起来是很重要的。
en: It is important to connect it with what we already know.

zh: 此外，我们可以尝试将所学内容复述给其他人。
en: Also, we can try to retell what we have learnt to a few others.

zh: 因此，增加我们记忆量的黄金法则之一是定期复习材料，尤其是在学习后的第一天。
en: Therefore, one of the golden rules to increase how much we remember is to review the material periodically, especially during the first day after learning.

zh: 学习后不久进行这种“间隔复习”有助于建立更强的记忆，并且比等到考试前才复习所有内容更有效。
en: This “spaced review" soon after learning helps build stronger memories and it is more effective than waiting to review everything before exams.

zh: 就你的记忆而言，你正处在一个好年纪。
en: You are at a good age in terms of your memory.

zh: 好好利用它！
en: Make good use of it!

zh: 由于我们大多数人没有像他们那样惊人的记忆力，在记忆详细的学习材料时，我们只需要关注重要的想法并对所学内容保持好奇。
en: As most of us do not have amazing memories like them, when memorising detailed learning materials, we simply need to focus on the important ideas and be curious about what we learn.

zh: 对我们所学的内容提出问题也有助于记忆。
en: Asking questions about what we learn also helps with memorisation.

zh: 另一个有效的记忆技巧是将相似的想法或信息分组，以便它们可以轻松地与已知的事物联系起来。
en: Another effective technique to remember things is to group similar ideas or information together so that they can be easily connected to things that are already known.

zh: 复述事件有助于将经历固定在记忆中。
en: Retelling events helps fix experiences in our memories.

zh: 确实有一些人拥有惊人的记忆力。
en: There are some people who do have amazing memories.

zh: 他们两人都擅长在有限的时间内记住特定的事物。
en: They are both good at remembering particular things for a limited time.

zh: 提高我们记忆量的黄金法则之一是定期复习材料。
en: One of the golden rules to increase how much we remember is to review the material periodically.

zh: 睡得好：如果你睡眠不足，你会“丢失”许多记忆。
en: Sleep well: If you don't sleep properly, you "lose" many of your memories.

zh: 进行体育锻炼：当你锻炼时，更多的氧气进入大脑，使你的记忆力更好。
en: Do physical exercise: When you exercise, more oxygen goes to your brain and makes your memory work better.

zh: 进行脑力锻炼：像锻炼身体一样锻炼大脑很重要。
en: Do mental exercise: It is important to exercise your brain just like your body.

zh: 新的活动比熟悉的活动更具挑战性。
en: New activities are more challenging than familiar ones.

zh: 保持兴趣：对自己正在学习的内容提出问题。
en: Be interested: Ask yourself questions about what you are learning.

zh: 如果我们感兴趣，我们会学得更好。
en: We learn better if we are interested.

zh: 形成心理图像：例如，如果你想记住“John Keys”这个名字，想象他的脸上有一把大钥匙。
en: Form a mental picture: For example, if you want to remember the name "John Keys", imagine his face with a big key on it.

zh: 图像越奇怪越好！
en: The stranger the picture, the better!

zh: 我对“记忆的秘密”的反思帮助我更好地理解了记忆是如何工作的。
en: My Reflection on "The Secrets of Your Memory" has helped me better understand how my memory works.

zh: 在阅读这篇文章之前，我不知道关于我们的记忆有这么多有趣的事情。
en: Before reading the text, I didn't know that there are so many interesting things about our memory.

zh: 现在我明白了为什么我们记得童年的事件比最近发生的事件更清楚。
en: Now I understand why we remember the events in childhood better than those that happened recently.

zh: 我还了解到有些人拥有惊人的记忆力。
en: I also learnt that some people have amazing memories.

zh: 这些人能比大多数人更好地记住事情。
en: These people can remember things better than most others.

zh: 对我来说，很高兴知道没有人拥有过目不忘的记忆力。
en: For me, it’s good to know that no one has a photographic memory.

zh: 也就是说，我们大多数人在试图记住事情时都必须付出努力。
en: That is to say, most of us will have to make an effort when trying to remember things.

zh: 更重要的是，了解一个人的记忆如何随着年龄变化是令人惊奇的。
en: What's more, it is amazing to know how a person's memory changes with age.

zh: 我们的记忆力在25岁时达到顶峰！
en: Our memory reaches its full power at the age of 25!

zh: 这意味着我的记忆力目前正处于最佳状态。
en: This means my memory is at its best state at the moment.

zh: 我应该充分利用它。
en: I should make the best use of it.

zh: 了解我们记忆的秘密不仅意味着学习关于它的事实，也意味着学习提高它的方法。
en: Getting to know the secrets of our memory means learning not only the facts about it, but also the ways to improve it.

zh: 我想我会遵循所提供的建议，并努力加强我的记忆力。
en: I think I will follow the advice provided and try to strengthen my memory.

zh: 在获取新知识时，我会尝试与我已学的东西建立联系。
en: When acquiring new knowledge, I'll try to make connections with what I have already learnt.

zh: 此外，我还必须学会进行“间隔复习”，尤其是在学习后的第一天。
en: Moreover, I must also learn to do “spaced review”, especially during the first day after learning.

zh: 例如，这意味着在学习后不久和睡觉前复习我学过的东西。
en: For example, this means going over what I've learnt soon after learning and before going to bed.

zh: 此外，为我需要做的工作制定计划对我来说很重要。
en: In addition, it is important for me to make plans for the work I need to do.

zh: 我相信这些方法将帮助我改善我的学习。
en: I'm sure these methods will help me improve my learning.

zh: 生活在国外听起来很令人兴奋，但适应另一种文化并不总是容易的。
en: Living in a foreign country sounds exciting, but it's not always easy to fit in with another culture.

zh: 这些中国学生正在国外大学学习。
en: These Chinese students are studying at universities abroad.

zh: 我喜欢美国文化的许多方面，但我不喜欢美国食物。
en: I like many things about American culture, but I don't like American food.

zh: 起初，我早餐吃玉米片，午餐吃汉堡包，晚餐吃披萨。
en: At first, I had cornflakes for breakfast, a hamburger for lunch, and pizza for dinner.

zh: 食物味道不错，但几天后，我感到恶心。
en: The food tasted good, but after a couple of days, I felt sick.

zh: 现在我在家做饭或者去当地的中餐馆吃晚饭。
en: Now I cook at home or have dinner in a local Chinese restaurant.

zh: 我发现在这里很难交到朋友。
en: I've found it difficult to make friends here.

zh: 澳大利亚学生看起来很友好，但他们只谈论我觉得无聊的事情，比如足球和冲浪。
en: The Australian students seem friendly, but they only talk about things which I find boring, like football and surfing.

zh: 我喜欢谈论中文流行音乐，但我还没有找到任何当地人有同样的兴趣。
en: I like to talk about C-pop, but I haven't found anyone local who shares that interest.

zh: 实际上，我注意到很多中国学生似乎只和他们的中国朋友待在一起。
en: Actually, I've noticed that a lot of Chinese students seem to only spend time with their Chinese friends.

zh: 英国的老师们很棒。
en: The teachers here in the UK are great.

zh: 我的数学老师说我是他最好的学生，我正在学到很多东西。
en: My maths teacher says I'm his best student and I'm learning a lot.

zh: 但有些科目非常困难，因为我必须用英语阅读和写作。
en: But some subjects are very difficult because I have to read and write in English.

zh: 课堂上也有很多讨论和辩论，我们的老师希望我们就所学的内容提出问题。
en: There are also a lot of discussions and debates in class, and our teachers expect us to ask questions about what we're learning.

zh: 我的英语口语正在提高，但有时我仍然感到相当害羞。
en: My spoken English is improving, but I still feel quite shy sometimes.

zh: 来中国的外国学生有时也会对中国文化的某些方面感到困扰。
en: Foreign students who visit China sometimes struggle with aspects of Chinese culture, too.

zh: 我在北京的一所语言学院学习了几个月。
en: I spent a few months studying at a language college in Beijing.

zh: 我很惊讶在北京遇到了那么多会说英语的人。
en: I was surprised by how many people I met in Beijing who could speak English.

zh: 我真的很想一有机会就练习说普通话，但我遇到的人通常更热衷于练习他们的英语，而不是帮助我练习中文！
en: I really wanted to practise speaking putonghua whenever I had a chance, but the people I met were often more interested in practising their English than in helping me practise my Chinese!

zh: 在我中国游学的第一个晚上，我们班去了一家大型海鲜餐厅吃晚饭。
en: On the first night of my study tour in China, our class went to a big seafood restaurant for dinner.

zh: 大家分享食物，这很好，但我习惯点自己的餐。
en: Everyone shared the food, which was nice, but I'm used to ordering my own meal.

zh: 而且，在晚餐结束时，每个人都想付账！
en: Also, at the end of the dinner, everyone wanted to pay the bill!

zh: 在我的国家，我们通常分摊账单，每个人支付自己的份额。
en: In my country, we usually split the bill and everyone pays their own share.

zh: 在我拜访了一位中国朋友的家庭之后，我朋友的祖父陪我走到了公交车站。
en: After my visit to a Chinese friend's family, my friend's grandfather walked me to the bus station.

zh: 我一直告诉他我自己认识路，但他坚持要带我去那里。
en: I kept telling him that I knew the way myself, but he insisted on taking me there.

zh: 他真是太好了。
en: That was very kind of him.

zh: 我认为这在许多其他文化中是不会发生的。
en: I don't think this would happen in many other cultures.

zh: 国际学生评估项目（PISA）是一项由经合组织（OECD）开展的全球性研究，针对15岁在校学生在数学、科学和阅读方面的学业表现。
en: The Programme for International Student Assessment (PISA) is a worldwide study of 15-year-old school pupils’ scholastic performance in mathematics, science, and reading undertaken by the OECD.

zh: 仔细研究阅读测试，揭示了一个有趣的事实：女孩在阅读方面表现优于男孩，这与女孩对阅读的更大喜爱有关。
en: A closer look at the reading tests, shows the interesting fact that girls are outperforming boys in reading and that this is associated with girls greater enjoyment of reading.

zh: 对阅读表现有重大影响的一个关键领域是，为享受乐趣而阅读，而不是仅仅为了学习或学校作业。
en: One key area which has a major impact on reading performance, is reading for enjoyment rather than simply for study or school work purposes.

zh: 有趣的是，PISA研究发现，在所有被评估的国家（除韩国外），女孩为享受乐趣而阅读的比例高于男孩。
en: Interestingly, the PISA study found that in all countries assessed (apart from South Korea), girls read for enjoyment more than boys.

zh: 研究发现，在所有被评估的国家中，只有略多于一半的男孩（52%），但几乎四分之三的女孩（73%）表示他们为享受乐趣而阅读，这可能让许多人感到惊讶。
en: The study found that in all the countries assessed, just over half of boys (52%), but almost three-quarters of girls (73%) said that they read for enjoyment which may come as a surprise to many.

zh: 在爱沙尼亚、拉脱维亚、立陶宛和荷兰等国，男孩和女孩之间的差异最为显著，他们发现性别之间存在30%或更大的差距。
en: In countries such as Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania and the Netherlands, the difference between boys and girls was most significant, where they found a 30% gap or more between the genders.

zh: 此外，在奥地利和列支敦士登等地，表示对阅读感兴趣的男孩比例非常低（40%或更低），这是令人担忧的统计数据。
en: Additionally in places such as Austria and Liechtenstein, very low levels of boys expressed interest in reading (40% or less), which are worrying statistics.

zh: 然而，并非所有国家都有如此明显的趋势。
en: However, not all countries had such an obvious trend.

zh: 在日本等地，男孩和女孩为享受乐趣而阅读的比例几乎相同，有54%的男孩和58%的女孩报告说他们喜欢阅读。
en: In places like Japan, boys and girls read for enjoyment at an almost identical level, with 54% of boys and 58% of girls reporting that they enjoy reading.

zh: 还有一些国家和地区在男孩的阅读乐趣方面得分非常高，例如在阿尔巴尼亚、印度尼西亚、哈萨克斯坦、吉尔吉斯斯坦、中国上海和泰国，至少有80%的男孩喜欢阅读，尽管这些积极的数字被报告了更高阅读乐趣水平（90%或更高）的女孩所匹配和超越。
en: And there were a few countries and regions that scored very highly in boys’ reading enjoyment, for example in Albania, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Shanghai in China and Thailand where at least 80% of boys enjoy reading, although these positive numbers are matched and passed by girls who reported an even higher level of reading enjoyment (90% or higher).

zh: PISA研究考察的一个有趣领域是女孩和男孩通常喜欢的文学类型。
en: An interesting area which the PISA study looked at was the types of literature that both girls and boys generally enjoy.

zh: 在决定阅读内容时，男孩和女孩的选择存在明显差异。
en: There were clear differences in the choices boys and girls make when deciding on what to read.

zh: 研究发现，男孩比女孩更喜欢阅读杂志、报纸和漫画书。
en: Boys were found to enjoy reading magazines, newspapers and comic books more than girls.

zh: 研究发现，女孩喜欢小说作品的可能性是男孩的两倍。
en: Girls were found to be twice as likely to enjoy works of fiction than boys.

zh: 与阅读乐趣不同，这种模式在所有国家都明显重复出现，尤其是在男孩阅读杂志和漫画书方面。
en: Unlike reading enjoyment, this pattern was clearly repeated across all countries, especially in terms of boys’ reading of magazines and comic books.

zh: 虽然各种形式的阅读肯定是有益的，并且应该受到鼓励，但为享受乐趣而阅读的男孩数量少，以及当他们阅读时选择有限，都证明了一个需要解决的问题。
en: While reading in all forms is certainly beneficial and should be encouraged, the low numbers of boys reading for enjoyment and their limited choices when they do, is evidence of an issue that needs to be addressed.

zh: 各国需要思考如何激发男孩的阅读兴趣，并确保他们从小就能接触到各种不同类型的文学作品。
en: Countries need to think about ways to inspire interest for boys in reading and ensure that they are exposed to a wide variety of different types of literature from a young age.

zh: 鉴于在经合组织国家中，三分之二的男孩仅为消遣而阅读报纸，这证明还有工作要做。
en: Given that in OECD countries, two in three boys only read newspapers for pleasure proves that there is work to be done.

zh: PISA研究有助于揭示在阅读方面存在的明显性别模式。
en: The PISA study has helped show a clear pattern in genders when it comes to reading.

zh: 对阅读兴趣或享受程度低可能对未来的生活成功产生长期影响。
en: Low levels of interest in or enjoyment of reading can have long term effects on future success in life.

zh: 因此，政府、教师和家长都需要努力寻找机会和策略来提高阅读熟练度水平，这一点很重要。
en: For this reason, it is important that governments, teachers and parents all work to identify opportunities and strategies to raise reading proficiency levels.

zh: 幸运的是，像PISA这样的研究提供了明确的证据，可以据此采取行动改善所有年轻人的成果。
en: Fortunately, studies such as PISA provide clear evidence that can be acted on to improve outcomes for all young people.

zh: 既然我现在是一名作家，人们经常问我关于开始写作的最佳年龄的意见。
en: Now that I am a writer, people often ask my opinion about the best age to start writing.

zh: 嗯，当我上中学时，我开始写一个在线博客：我发布了我对学习、社会问题和学校生活的思考。
en: Well, when I was in secondary school I started writing an online blog: I posted reflections I had on learning, social issues and school life.

zh: 有时，我的同学就我的帖子与我争论，而其他人则同意我的观点。
en: Sometimes, my classmates argued with me about my posts, while others agreed with my ideas.

zh: 结果，我的博客变得如此受欢迎，以至于它成为了我们学校课后谈话的基础。
en: As a result, my blog became so popular that it became the base of after-class talks in my school.

zh: 我的老师们对此很好奇并阅读了它。
en: My teachers were curious about it and read it.

zh: 我的校长甚至告诉我，阅读我的博客给他留下了深刻的印象，并帮助他了解了学生生活的许多方面。
en: My principal even told me that reading my blog had left a deep impression on him and helped him understand many aspects of students’ lives.

zh: 然而，他也建议我更专注于我的学习！
en: However, he also suggested that I focus more on my studies!

zh: 当我第一次尝试写小说时，我苦苦思索一个故事，但信不信由你，它最终是基于我的一篇博客文章。
en: When I first attempted to write a novel, I struggled to think of a story, but believe it or not, it ended up being based on one of my blog posts.

zh: 作为一名英语老师，我想分享一些关于如何学好英语的建议。
en: As an English teacher, I'd like to share some of my advice on ways to learn English well.

zh: 首先，你需要尽可能多地练习，不仅在学校里，而且在课堂之外也要练习。
en: Firstly, you need to practise as often as possible, not just in school but beyond the classroom too.

zh: 例如，尝试阅读不仅仅是课本。
en: For example, try reading more than just the coursebooks.

zh: 有很多为年轻人写的英语文学作品。
en: There's lots of English literature that is written for young people.

zh: 其次，如果你对不熟悉的单词有困难，试着思考这些单词被说出的语境。
en: Secondly, if you are having trouble with unfamiliar words, try thinking about the context in which the words are spoken.

zh: 那可能会帮助你理解其含义。
en: That might help you figure out the meaning.

zh: 语法对一些学生来说可能是一个大难题。
en: Grammar can be a big headache for some students.

zh: 不幸的是，仅仅记住规则是不够的。
en: Unfortunately, just memorising the rules is not enough.

zh: 我建议学习规则，并且在练习时不要担心犯小错误。
en: I would recommend learning the rules and not worrying if you make small mistakes while practising.

zh: 它会及时到来，慢慢地你将在不知不觉中开始掌握这门语言。
en: It will come in time and slowly you will begin to acquire the language without even realising it.

zh: 我希望你们都发现我的建议有帮助。
en: I hope you all find my tips helpful.

zh: 祝你在英语学习中好运！
en: Good luck in your English language learning!

zh: 所以李珍，我决定明年要去南非学习！
en: So Li Zhen, I've decided that I'm going to study in South Africa next year!

zh: 非洲南部的哪个部分？
en: Which part of southern Africa?

zh: 不，是南非，那个国家。
en: No, South Africa, the country.

zh: 我已经报名了开普敦大学，我打算和我父母的一个朋友住在一起，他住在城里。
en: I've enrolled in the University of Cape Town and I'm going to stay with a friend of my parents’, who lives in the city.

zh: 你报名了特定的课程吗？
en: Have you enrolled in a particular course?

zh: 是的，他们有一个研究部门，我可以在那里研究非洲野生动物。
en: Yes, they have a research department where I can study African wildlife.

zh: 前几天我和一位大学教授通了电话。
en: I spoke to a university professor on the phone the other day.

zh: 听他发音的方式很有趣。
en: It was interesting to hear his way of pronouncing words.

zh: 他非常友好，所以我迫不及待地想去那里。
en: He was very friendly, so I can't wait to get over there.

zh: 嗯，我迫不及待地想去看你。
en: Well, I can't wait to visit you.

zh: 威廉·莎士比亚（1564-1616）是英语语言最伟大的作家之一。
en: William Shakespeare (1564-1616) is one of the greatest writers of the English language.

zh: 他出生在英格兰埃文河畔的斯特拉特福。
en: He was born in Stratford-upon-Avon, in England.

zh: 到1592年，他已在伦敦成为著名的演员和作家。
en: By 1592, he had become famous in London as an actor and writer.

zh: 在接下来的20年里，他写了37部戏剧和许多著名的诗歌。
en: Over the next 20 years, he wrote 37 plays and many famous poems.

zh: 莎士比亚早期的大部分戏剧是历史剧（关于真实国王和战争的戏剧）和喜剧（通常关于婚姻和爱情的有趣戏剧）。
en: Most of Shakespeare's early plays were history plays (plays about real-life kings and wars) and comedies (funny plays often about marriage and love).

zh: 在他的晚年，他写了许多他最伟大的戏剧，这些戏剧涉及更黑暗的主题，如邪恶、死亡和背叛。
en: In his later years, he wrote many of his greatest plays which dealt with darker themes like evil, death and betrayal.

zh: 许多人认为《李尔王》是莎士比亚最好的戏剧之一。
en: Many people think that King Lear is one of Shakespeare's best plays.

zh: 这是一个关于疯狂、死亡和痛苦的故事。
en: It is a story of madness, death and suffering.

zh: 李尔王年老但不明智。
en: King Lear is old but not wise.

zh: 他爱他那两个 secretly 恨他的女儿。
en: He loves his two daughters who secretly hate him.

zh: 他恨那个唯一真正爱他的女儿。
en: He hates the only daughter who really loves him.

zh: 随着故事的发展，他开始意识到自己犯了一个可怕的错误。
en: As the story develops, he comes to realise that he has made a terrible mistake.

zh: 李尔，不列颠国王 奥尔巴尼公爵，高纳里的丈夫
en: Lear, King of Britain Duke of Albany, Goneril's husband

zh: 高纳里，李尔的长女 康沃尔公爵，里根的丈夫
en: Goneril, Lear's oldest daughter Duke of Cornwall, Regan's husband

zh: 里根，李尔的二女儿 法兰西国王
en: Regan, Lear's second daughter King of France

zh: 第七场 李尔王宫殿中的一个大房间
en: Scene 7 A large room in King Lear's palace

zh: 李尔王走进来，后面跟着康沃尔公爵、奥尔巴尼公爵、高纳里、里根、科迪莉亚和一个拿着地图的仆人。
en: King Lear comes in, followed by the Duke of Cornwall, the Duke of Albany, Goneril, Regan, Cordelia and a servant carrying a map.

zh: 仆人把地图在桌子上摊开，李尔、康沃尔和奥尔巴尼站在地图周围。
en: The servant opens the map on a table and Lear, Cornwall and Albany stand around it.

zh: 我已经把我的王国分成了三部分，因为我老了。
en: I have broken my kingdom into three parts because I am getting old.

zh: 我亲爱的康沃尔和奥尔巴尼，我会告诉你们我打算给你们妻子，也就是我的女儿们什么。
en: My dear Cornwall and Albany, I will tell you what I plan to give your wives, my daughters.

zh: 法兰西国王想娶我最小的女儿，他已经在宫廷里耐心地等待我的答复了。
en: The King of France wants to marry my youngest daughter, and he has waited patiently at court for my answer.

zh: 亲爱的女儿们，我打算把我所有的土地和烦恼都留给你们。
en: Dear daughters, I plan to leave you all my land and worries.

zh: 但在此之前，你们谁最爱我？
en: But before that which of you loves me the most?

zh: 我将把我王国最大的部分给那个能表现出对我最大爱的女儿。
en: I will give the largest part of my kingdom to the daughter who can show the greatest love for me.

zh: 高纳里，我最年长的孩子，你可以先说。
en: Goneril, my oldest child, you can speak first.

zh: 陛下，我爱您如同爱我自己的生命、我的健康和我的荣誉一样多——就像任何孩子爱父亲一样多。
en: Sir, I love you as much as I love my own life, my health and my honour — as much as any child has ever loved a father.

zh: 我该说什么呢？我的爱是真实的，但我无法用这样的语言来描述它！
en: What shall I say? My love is real, but I can’t describe it in words like these!

zh: 这片土地将永远属于你、你的丈夫和你的孩子们。
en: This land will belong to you, your husband and your children forever.

zh: 现在，我的二女儿，我最亲爱的里根，怎么说？
en: Now, what does my second daughter, my dearest Regan, say?

zh: 我爱您和我的姐姐一样多，但她说的还不够。
en: I love you as much as my sister, but she hasn't said enough.

zh: 除了我对您的爱，什么都不重要。
en: Nothing matters except my love for you.

zh: 那是我唯一的幸福！
en: That is my only happiness!

zh: 我不能用这样的语言！
en: I can’t use language like this!

zh: 但那又有什么关系呢？
en: But what does that matter?

zh: 真爱比空话更重要。
en: True love is more important than empty words.

zh: 我把这里的这片土地都给你、你的丈夫和你未来的孩子们。
en: I am giving you, your husband and future children all this land here.

zh: 现在，我美丽的小女儿，你能说些什么来得到比你姐姐们更多的土地呢？
en: Now, my beautiful, youngest daughter, what can you say to get more land than your sisters?

zh: 考狄利娅：[轻声地] 没有什么，我的主上。
en: Cordelia: [quietly] Nothing, my Lord.

zh: 李尔：没有什么只会给你带来一无所有。再说。
en: Lear: Nothing will bring you nothing. Speak again.

zh: 考狄利娅：我很抱歉，但我无法用言语表达我的感情。我爱您，就像一个孩子应该爱她的父亲那样——仅此而已。
en: Cordelia: I am sorry, but I can’t put my feelings into words. I love you as a child should love a father — that is all.

zh: 李尔：[严肃地] 你会后悔的，考狄利娅，如果你说不出比这更好的话。
en: Lear: [seriously] You will be sorry, Cordelia, if you can’t make a better speech than that.

zh: 考狄利娅：您给了我生命、您的爱和您的保护。所以，我当然也爱您作为回报。我的姐姐们说她们把全部的爱都给了您。那她们为什么还有丈夫呢？当我结婚时，我会把我一半的爱给我的丈夫。我永远不会像我的姐姐们那样，把我全部的爱都给我的父亲。
en: Cordelia: You gave me my life, your love and your protection. So, of course, I love you in return. My sisters say that you have all their love. So why do they have husbands? When I marry, I shall give my husband half my love. I will never be like my sisters, giving my father all my love.

zh: 李尔：[悲伤地] 你真的是这个意思吗？作为一个年轻女子，你太不近人情了。
en: Lear: [sadly] Do you really mean this? For a young woman, you are very unkind.

zh: 考狄利娅：我很年轻，我的主上，但我是诚实的。
en: Cordelia: I am young, my Lord, but honest.

zh: 李尔：[愤怒地] 诚实？那我也对你诚实！从今天起，我不再是你的父亲。你对我以及我的心来说将是一个陌生人！康沃尔和奥尔巴尼，你们的妻子可以得到考狄利娅的那部分王国。人们仍然会称我为国王，但我所有土地的治理权将是你们的。
en: Lear: [angrily] Honest? Then I will be honest with you! From today, I won't be your father. You will be a stranger to me and my heart! Cornwall and Albany, your wives can have Cordelia’s part of the kingdom. People will still call me king, but the government of all my lands will be yours.

zh: 李尔：[对法国国王说] 伟大的国王，我不希望你爱一个我恨的人。你应该找一个更好的女人。
en: Lear: [to the King of France] Great King, I don’t want you to love a person that I hate. You should look for a better woman.

zh: 法国国王：[困惑地] 五分钟前她还是你最宠爱的女儿。她做了什么可怕的事吗？还是你爱她不如我想象的那么多？
en: King of France: [confused] Five minutes ago she was your favourite daughter. Has she done something terrible? Or do you love her less than I thought?

zh: 考狄利娅：[对李尔说] 告诉国王我没有做任何可怕的事。我不像我姐姐们那样有一张不诚实的嘴。这就是我失去父亲之爱的原因。
en: Cordelia: [to Lear] Tell the King that I have done nothing terrible. I don’t have a dishonest tongue like my sisters. That is why I have lost my father’s love.

zh: 法国国王：就这些吗？天生的羞怯常常让真心话说不出口。
en: King of France: Is that all? A natural shyness often leaves a true heart's words unspoken.

zh: 法国国王：[深情地对考狄利娅说] 最美丽的考狄利娅，我更加爱你，因为你的父亲拒绝收留你。我将欣然让你成为我心中的王后和我国家的王后。你即将离开这个家，但你会找到一个更好的家。
en: King of France: [lovingly to Cordelia] Fairest Cordelia, I love you more because your father refuses to keep you. I will happily make you the Queen of my heart and Queen of my country. You are leaving this house, but you will find a better home.

zh: 李尔：[不耐烦地] 你可以拥有她。她不是我的女儿了，我永远不想再看到她的脸。
en: Lear: [impatiently] You can have her. She isn’t my daughter, and I never want to see her face again.

zh: 去年夏天，我决定在假期里和一位著名艺术家一起上额外的艺术课。
en: Last summer, I decided to take extra art classes during the holidays with a well-known artist.

zh: 她是一位有才华的画家，尽管她还没有创作出任何杰作。
en: She was a talented painter, although she had not produced any work of masterpiece yet.

zh: 我被告知她的一些画作曾在艺术展览中展出。
en: I was told some of her paintings had been put on display in art exhibitions.

zh: 我真的很喜欢她的作品，因为她画中的场景很神秘。
en: I really loved her work because the scene in her paintings was mysterious.

zh: 她是一位鼓舞人心的老师，她挑战我们去尝试不同的艺术形式。
en: She was an inspiring teacher who challenged us to experiment with different art forms.

zh: 我发现绘画和油画很容易，但其他形式更具挑战性。
en: I found drawing and painting easy but the other forms were more challenging.

zh: 我对观众的反应感到好奇，所以我在夜空下的花丛中画了一个强大的人物。
en: I was curious about viewers’ reactions so I painted a powerful figure beneath a night sky in a bed of flowers.

zh: 我的老师说这是一个平静的场景。
en: My teacher said it was a calming scene.

zh: 京剧是中国的国粹。
en: Beijing Opera is a national treasure of China.

zh: 演员扮演四种角色之一：生，男性角色；旦，女性角色；丑，小丑角色和净角色。
en: Actors play one of four roles: sheng, male roles; dan, female roles; chou, clown roles and jing roles.

zh: 在早期，京剧最常在市场、街道、茶馆或寺庙外的露天舞台上表演。
en: In the early days, Beijing Opera was most often performed on open-air stages in markets, streets, teahouses or outside temples.

zh: 音乐必须大声演奏，表演者必须用尖锐的声音唱歌，以便在人群中听到。
en: The music had to be played loudly and performers had to sing with sharp voices, in order to be heard over the crowd.

zh: 他们必须穿着色彩鲜艳的服装，以吸引观众的注意力。
en: They had to wear bright costumes in colourful patterns to attract the audience's attention.

zh: 象征在京剧中扮演着非常重要的角色。
en: Symbols play a very important role in Beijing Opera.

zh: 许多身体动作可以代表复杂的行动。
en: Many body movements can represent complex actions.

zh: 例如，当一位女士乘坐马车时，表演者实际上是在两边各举一面旗子行走。
en: For example, when a lady is riding in a carriage, the performer actually walks with a flag on each side.

zh: 四个将军和四个士兵代表千军万马。
en: Four generals and four soldiers refer to an army of thousands.

zh: 换句话说，京剧中的每一个动作和移动都很重要。
en: In other words, every action and movement in Beijing Opera is important.

zh: 古典音乐是欧洲宫廷和大型音乐厅的音乐。
en: Classical music was the music of European courts and big concert halls.

zh: 具体来说，古典音乐指的是18世纪和19世纪初的管弦乐。
en: Specifically, classical music refers to orchestral music of the 18th and early 19th century.

zh: 古典音乐用传统的欧洲乐器演奏，通常是在大型管弦乐队中。
en: Classical music is played with traditional European instruments, often in large orchestras.

zh: 贝多芬和莫扎特是两位最著名的古典作曲家。
en: Beethoven and Mozart are two of the most famous classical composers.

zh: 许多人喜爱古典音乐，因为它有丰富优美的旋律。
en: Classical music is enjoyed by many for its rich and beautiful melodies.

zh: 布鲁斯音乐最初是被带到美国的非洲奴隶的音乐。
en: Blues started off as the music of the African slaves who were brought to the United States.

zh: 它与西非音乐密切相关。
en: It is closely related to the music of West Africa.

zh: 它通过马迪·沃特斯和B.B.金等音乐家而在全世界闻名。
en: It became well-known throughout the world through musicians such as Muddy Waters and B.B. King.

zh: 在许多方面，布鲁斯仍然是当今许多流行曲调中的重要元素。
en: In many ways, blues remains an important element in many of today’s popular tunes.

zh: 爵士乐源于布鲁斯。
en: Jazz grew out of blues.

zh: 和布鲁斯一样，爵士乐的重要元素之一是即兴创作。
en: As with blues, one of the important elements of jazz is improvisation.

zh: 即兴创作是指音乐家演奏未写成的音乐以契合场合的氛围。
en: Improvisation happens when a musician plays unwritten music to fit the mood of the occasion.

zh: 爵士乐中最著名的名字之一是迈尔斯·戴维斯。
en: One of the most famous names in jazz is Miles Davis.

zh: 他以总是寻找演奏爵士乐的新方式而闻名，对许多人来说，这代表了爵士乐的精神。
en: He was famous for always finding new ways of playing jazz, and for many people, this represented the spirit of jazz.

zh: 摇滚乐是从快节奏的布鲁斯发展而来的。
en: Rock'n’ roll developed from fast blues.

zh: 查克·贝里和埃尔维斯·普雷斯利是摇滚乐的大人物，而英国的披头士乐队则吸收了这种音乐，并从中创造出了他们自己的声音。
en: Chuck Berry and Elvis Presley were the big names in rock'n' roll and the Beatles in England took the music and made their own sounds from it.

zh: 当吉他声音变得更响亮、吉他独奏变得更长时，摇滚乐就变成了纯粹的摇滚。
en: When the guitars got louder and the guitar solos got longer, rock'n’ roll became just rock.

zh: 如今，摇滚是世界上最流行的音乐形式之一，其粉丝遍布世界各地。
en: Now rock is one of the most popular forms of music in the world and its fans can be found all over the world.

zh: 如今，这些音乐风格的名称对音乐家来说不那么重要了。
en: Today, these names of music styles are less important to musicians.

zh: 广播、电视和互联网使得声音更容易跨越文化传播，风格也变得更加混合。
en: Radio, television and the Internet have made it easier for sounds to travel across cultures, and styles have become more mixed.

zh: 如果说流行音乐的历史揭示了一件事，那就是优秀的音乐家总是会从他们听到的不同风格中创造出新的音乐。
en: If there is one thing the history of popular music has shown, it is that good musicians will always create new music from the different styles they hear.

zh: 多年来，科学家们一直报告称，我们人类正在影响和改变地球的气候。
en: For many years now, scientists have reported that we, as human beings, are affecting and changing the Earth's climate.

zh: 他们特别指出，在过去的50年里，从工厂和车辆排放到我们大气中的温室气体数量大幅增加，并且这种增加正在加速被称为全球变暖的过程。
en: They say that, in particular, the last 50 years has seen a huge increase in the quantity of greenhouse gases being released into our atmosphere from factories and vehicles, and that this increase is speeding up the process known as global warming.

zh: 科学家们认为全球变暖是迄今为止21世纪最大的环境问题。
en: Scientists believe global warming to be by far the biggest environmental problem of the 21st century.

zh: 那么它是什么，它如何构成危险，以及为什么它与我们相关？
en: So what is it, how is it a danger, and why is it relevant to us?

zh: 全球变暖是用来表示地球温度上升的术语，这反过来又导致地球气候的变化。
en: Global warming is the term used to indicate a rise in the Earth's temperature which, in turn, causes changes to the Earth's climate.

zh: 科学家们发现，大气中温室气体的积累，这些气体捕获来自太阳的热量，控制着温度。
en: Scientists have discovered that the accumulation of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere, which trap the heat from the Sun, control the temperature.

zh: 当温室气体增加时，更多的热量被捕获，地球的温度上升。
en: When the greenhouse gases increase, more heat is trapped and the Earth's temperature rises.

zh: 在过去的100年里，地球的温度已经上升了1华氏度。
en: The Earth's temperature has increased by one degree Fahrenheit in the last 100 years.

zh: 这很大程度上归因于人类活动，例如工业增加、农业、森林砍伐、交通增长和燃料燃烧。
en: Much of this has been attributed to human activities, such as increased industry, agriculture, the felling of forests, the rise in transport and the burning of fuels.

zh: 1华氏度可能听起来不多，但如果我们继续产生温室气体，如二氧化碳，我们将不得不面对其后果。
en: One degree Fahrenheit may not sound like much, but if we continue to produce greenhouse gases, such as carbon dioxide, we will have to face the repercussions.

zh: 科学家们警告说，全球变暖将导致灾难性的气候变化，包括更频繁的洪水、干旱和极端天气。
en: Scientists warn that global warming will cause catastrophic climate changes, including more frequent flooding, drought and extreme weather.

zh: 污染的增加还将导致严重的健康影响，并影响诸如农业等产业。
en: The increase in pollution will also cause serious health impacts and affect industries such as agriculture.

zh: 极地冰盖的融化将带来可怕的后果，海平面上升，导致低洼地区（如岛屿和沿海城市）发生洪水。
en: The melting of the polar ice caps will have dire consequences, with sea levels rising, causing flooding in low lying areas, such as islands and coastal cities.

zh: 那么，我们如何才能阻止这种情况发生呢？
en: So, how can we stop this from happening?

zh: 各地政府都有责任减少温室气体的产生。
en: Governments everywhere have a responsibility to reduce the production of greenhouse gases.

zh: 但我们也可以做出一些小的改变。
en: But we can make small changes too.

zh: 每个人都需要尽自己的一份力。
en: Every person needs to play his / her part.

zh: 建议包括回收利用、使用公共交通工具、在家少用水、使用更节能的电器以及关灯。
en: Suggestions include recycling, using public transport, using less water at home, using more energy efficient appliances and switching off lights.

zh: 个人的小小改变将累积成全球的巨大变化。
en: Small personal changes will add up to big changes worldwide.

zh: 当你学习一个新单词时，在口语中使用它。
en: When you learn a new word, use it in spoken English.

zh: 如果你已经在对话中使用过单词，它能提高你记忆单词的能力。
en: It improves your ability to remember words if you've already used them in a conversation.

zh: 我同意说是记忆新单词和短语最有效的方法，但每个人都是不同的，所以要找到最适合你的方法。
en: I agree that speaking is the most way of remembering new words and phrases but everyone is different, so find the approach that works best for you.

zh: 当我们与某人交谈时，我们会使用各种表达方式向对方表明我们正在关注他/她所说的话。
en: When we have a conversation with someone, we use various expressions to show the other person that we are paying attention to what he / she is saying.

zh: 例如，如果有人告诉我们一些我们认为很吸引人或喜欢听的事情，我们可能会说：“哇！”
en: For example, if someone tells us about something that we find fascinating or enjoy hearing about, we may remark, "Wow!"

zh: 如果他们告诉我们不同国家中意思相同但完全不同的单词，我们可能会说：“它们听起来如此不同！”
en: If they tell us about totally different words that mean the same thing in different countries, we may say, "They sound so different!"

zh: 这些短语表明我们在倾听，并鼓励对方给我们更多细节。
en: These phrases show that we are listening and encourage the other person to give us more details.

zh: 在听到一些令人惊奇的事情后，我们可以这样回答：“真有趣！”
en: After hearing something amazing, we can then reply with, "Interesting!"

zh: 如果对方分享了一些我们不知道的事情，我们可能会说：“啊，我以前不知道那个！”
en: If the person shares something we didn't know, we may say, "Ah, I didn't know that!"

zh: 不要低估适当回应的重要性，以显示兴趣并尊重他人。
en: Don't underestimate the importance of an appropriate response to show interest and to be respectful to others.

zh: 想象一下，如果你正在和某人说话，而他们没有以适当的方式回应——你很快就会停止说话！
en: Imagine if you were speaking to someone and they didn't respond in an appropriate way — you would soon stop talking!

zh: 如今，越来越多不同年龄和背景的人为了提升自己而继续学习。
en: Nowadays, more and more people of various ages and backgrounds are continuing to study to improve themselves.

zh: 以下是一些人关于他们终身学习经历的看法。
en: Here's what some people have to say about their life-long learning experiences.

zh: "学习永远不嫌晚。"
en: "It's never too late to learn."

zh: 那是我高考失败后得到的建议。
en: That was the advice I got after I failed my college entrance exam.

zh: 现在我即将通过远程学习获得计算机工程学士学位并从大学毕业。
en: Now I'm about to graduate from university with a bachelor’s degree in Computer Engineering by distance learning.

zh: 我决定通过晚上和周末学习攻读学位来给自己第二次机会，同时保留一份稳定的全职工作。
en: I decided to give myself a second chance by studying for a degree in the evenings and on weekends, while keeping a secure full-time job.

zh: 坦率地说，过去四年很艰难，但这是值得的，因为我得到了一份新工作！
en: Frankly speaking, the last four years have been hard, but it’s been worth it as I have a new job offer!

zh: 三个月前，当我的经理告诉我我将被解雇时，我的大脑一片空白，心中充满了愤怒。
en: Three months ago, when my manager told me that I would be laid off, my mind went blank and my heart swelled with anger.

zh: 在过去，如果有人在一家公司工作了20多年，他们会一直待到退休！
en: In the past, if someone had worked for a company for over 20 years, they would have stayed there until retirement!

zh: 于是我决定专注于提升自我并追寻我的梦想，所以我报名参加了一个商业课程，现在已接近完成。
