en: I then decided to focus on improving myself and following my dreams, so I enrolled in a business course which I have nearly finished now.

zh: 我学到了很多新技能，并且已经为建立自己的出口公司准备了一份商业计划！
en: I have learnt so many new skills and have already prepared a business plan for setting up my own export company!

zh: 我认为被解雇将被证明是发生在我身上最好的事情。
en: I think that getting laid off will prove to be the best thing that ever happened to me.

zh: 我相信你们都知道这句谚语：“老狗学不会新把戏。”
en: I'm sure you all know the saying, “You can't teach an old dog new tricks.”

zh: 嗯，这当然不是真的！
en: Well, it’s certainly not true!

zh: 去年，我的女儿和家人搬到了美国，所以我决定是时候成为一名互联网用户，以便与他们保持联系。
en: Last year, my daughter moved to the US with her family, so I decided that it was time I became an Internet user in order to keep in touch with them.

zh: 我让我的孙子教我如何发送电子邮件和搜索网页，我现在非常喜欢这些。
en: I got my grandson to show me how to send an email and search the web, which I now love.

zh: 能够和我的孙辈视频聊天也非常棒。
en: It’s also wonderful to be able to video chat with my grandchildren.

zh: 很多人认为退休后，你能做的只是关闭大脑，埋葬你所学的一切，但事实并非如此。
en: So many people think that after you retire, all you can do is switch off your brain and bury everything you've ever learnt, but that doesn’t have to be the case.

zh: 名词从句是在句中起名词作用的从句。
en: A noun clause is a clause that functions as a noun.

zh: 每个名词从句至少有一个主语和谓语。
en: Every noun clause has at least a Subject and a predicate.

zh: 名词从句是从属性句子，在句中作主语、宾语、表语或同位语。
en: Noun clauses are dependent clauses that fill noun phrase slots in a sentence, such as subject, object, predicative or appositive.

zh: 引导名词从句的引导词是：
en: The words that can introduce noun clauses are as follows.

zh: 我现在最想做的就是开车出去兜风。
en: What I feel like doing most now is going out for a drive.

zh: 他是否会接受这份工作仍然未知。
en: Whether he'll take the job is still unknown.

zh: 他们要把办公室搬到哪里还不清楚。
en: Where they'll move their office to is not clear.

zh: 他什么时候去纽约还没有决定。
en: When he'll leave for New York hasn't been decided.

zh: 很明显，他非常享受他的家庭生活。
en: It's obvious that he enjoys his family life very much.

zh: 他不尊重他的父母，这是一种耻辱。
en: It's a shame that he doesn't respect his parents.

zh: 我们的父母是否理解和支持你非常重要。
en: It matters greatly whether our parents understand and support you.

zh: 在句中it是形式主语，真正的主语是名词从句。
en: The word it is the grammatical subject of the sentence, taking the place of the noun clause that is the true subject of the sentence.

zh: 即使没有it，句子在语法上也是正确的。
en: The sentence would be equally grammatical without using it.

zh: 不过这种结构比较正式，在口语中不太常见。
en: However, this structure is more formal and less common in spoken English.

zh: 我希望人们能在教育上花更多的钱。
en: I hope that people will spend more money on education.

zh: 你知道比尔支持哪个足球队吗？
en: Do you know which football team Bill supports?

zh: 你介意告诉我你穿什么鞋码吗？
en: Would you mind telling me what shoe size you take?

zh: 我想知道我是否应该接受这个提议。
en: I wonder whether I should accept the offer or not.

zh: 他问我是否可以教他如何操作空调。
en: He asked if I could show him how to operate the air conditioner.

zh: 选择正确的自行车取决于你想用它做什么。
en: Choosing the right bike depends on what you want to use it for.

zh: 他的建议是我们应该雇用更有经验的人。
en: His suggestion is that we should hire more experienced people.

zh: 那就是我们应该做的。
en: That's what we should do.

zh: 那就是他们成功的方式。
en: That is how they succeeded.

zh: 这就是他上学迟到的原因。
en: That is why he was late for school.

zh: 那是我妈妈住院的时候。
en: That was when my mum was in hospital.

zh: 事情并不像我们预期的那样。
en: Things were not as we had expected.

zh: 动词-ing形式在句中起形容词或副词的作用。
en: Verb-ing form acts as an adjective or an adverb in a sentence.

zh: 我们可以将动词-ing形式用作定语、状语、宾语补足语或表语。
en: We can use verb-ing forms as attributive, adverbial, object complement or predicative of sentences.

zh: 那个哭泣的孩子膝盖上有个伤口。
en: The crying child had a cut on his knee.

zh: 市场上挤满了交易和讨价还价的人。
en: The market is filled with people trading and bargaining.

zh: 当我走过房间时，我听到两个人在争吵。
en: As I walked past the room, I heard two people arguing.

zh: 这场足球比赛很激动人心。
en: The football game was exciting.

zh: 我们有时用动词-ing形式代替定语或状语从句。
en: We sometimes use verb-ing forms instead of relative and adverbial clauses.

zh: 我们也可以用动词-ing形式代替由and或but连接的两个并列句。
en: We can also use verb-ing forms instead of two main clauses joined by and or but.

zh: 到达学校时，孩子们发现学校关门了。
en: Arriving at the school, the children found it was closed.

zh: 你见过管理公司的老板吗？
en: Have you met the boss managing the company?

zh: 汽车穿过城市，驶向山区。
en: The car drove through the city, heading for the mountain area.

zh: 动词-ing形式作状语时，其逻辑主语必须与句子的主语一致。
en: The subject of verb-ing form must also be the subject of the main verb.

zh: 搬着一个又大又重的箱子，她在台阶上绊了一下摔倒了。
en: Carrying a large and heavy box, she tripped on a step and fell.

zh: 下面这个句子是错误的。
en: The following sentence is not correct.

zh: 我们在句中使用动词-ed形式作为形容词或副词。
en: We use verb-ed forms as adjectives or adverbs in sentences.

zh: 当用作形容词时，它们可以作定语、宾语补足语、状语或表语。
en: When used as adjectives, they can serve as attributive, object complement, adverbial or predicative.

zh: 康复的动物将很快被放生。
en: The recovered animals will be released soon.

zh: 当他进入房间时，他发现窗户破了。
en: When he entered the room, he found the window broken.

zh: 这所房子建于1760年，已有200多年的历史。
en: Built in 1760, the house is over 200 years old.

zh: 街道两旁排列着小商店。
en: The street is lined with small shops.

zh: 他们停下来吃点心。
en: They stopped to have a snack.

zh: 我们停止了谈话，因为我们听到外面有奇怪的声音。
en: We stopped talking because we heard a strange noise outside.

zh: 我试图给你打电话，但打不通。
en: I tried to ring you but I couldn't get through.

zh: 今晚我试着烤面包。
en: I'll try baking bread tonight.

zh: 他知道有些人很聪明。
en: He knows that some people are smart.

zh: 他们知道有些人很聪明。
en: They know that some people are smart.

zh: 我们每天都在失去脑细胞。
en: We have been losing brain cells every day.

zh: 他一直在读这本书。
en: He has been reading the book.

zh: 那个男孩或他的朋友们每天都跑步。
en: The boy or his friends run every day.

zh: 他的朋友们或那个男孩每天都跑步。
en: His friends or the boy runs every day.

zh: 每个孩子都得到一本书来读。
en: Each of the children gets a book to read.

zh: 清晰地写下你的笔记能帮助你更好地记住事情。
en: Writing your notes clearly helps you remember the things better.

zh: 凡是艺术家都须有一半是诗人，一半是哲人。
en: All artists must be half-poet, half-craftsman.

zh: 蒙克在他的日记中谈到了灵感的来源。
en: In his diary, Munch talked about what inspired him.

zh: 太阳落山时，我和两个朋友一起走在路上; 天空突然变得像血一样红……
en: I was walking down the road with two friends when the sun set; suddenly the sky turned as red as blood...

zh: 剧院音乐指挥迈克尔·奥姆洛夫也走上台和他一起指挥乐队。
en: The theatre's musical director, Michael Umlauf, joined him and together the two men took charge of the orchestra.

zh: 在过去的几个月里，我们一直在做关于年轻球员对篮球兴趣的采访和拍摄。
en: For the past few months, we have been interviewing and filming young players about their interest in basketball.

zh: 我还想知道您能不能给我提供更多关于如何申请参加艺术节的信息。
en: I wonder if you could also give me more information about how to apply to be part of this festival.

zh: 万物并育而不相害，道并行而不相悖。
en: Living things that are nourished will not injure one another; roads that run parallel will not interfere with one another.

zh: 比方说你刷牙的时候让水哗哗地流，外出的时候不关灯，或者掉了一块垃圾却懒得捡起来。
en: Well, say you leave the tap running while you brush your teeth, leave a light on when you go out or you drop a piece of litter and can't be bothered to pick it up.

zh: 除了自己的科研，她还全身心地投入环保事业。
en: In addition to her research, she has been whole-heartedly committed to environmental protection.

zh: 她为什么要建立这样一个组织？该组织的目的是什么？
en: Why did she set up such an organisation and what is it for?

zh: 20世纪60年代一群骑行爱好者提出了一个想法。
en: In the 1960s, a group of cycling fans came up with an idea.

zh: 由于这个提议以及众多人的努力，如20世纪60年代的骑行爱好者……
en: Thanks to the ideas and efforts of many people, like the cycling fans of the 1960s...

zh: 由于你们帮忙，我们提前完成了任务。
en: Thanks to your help, we accomplished the task ahead of schedule.

zh: 去年8月，城市官员采访了大约500名市民，调查了解人们对环境保护的态度以及他们采取了什么行动。
en: Last August, city officials interviewed about 500 residents to find out people's attitudes to protecting the environment and what actions they had taken.

zh: 学而不思则罔，思而不学则殆。
en: He who learns but does not think is lost. He who thinks but does not learn is in great danger.

zh: 为了积极主动地学习，我建议做五件事。
en: I suggest doing five things to take an active role in your learning.

zh: 最终，你也许会同意说话人或是作者的观点。
en: You might end up agreeing with the speaker or writer after all.

zh: 他们努力从每个观点最核心的地方寻找真相。
en: They attempt to find the truth at the heart of each idea.

zh: 即使一个想法听起来完全不可能，其中也许有某个方面是基于事实的。
en: Even when an idea sounds entirely unlikely, there may be an aspect of it that is based on truth.

zh: 确实，我们难免会讨厌某些人，毕竟这是人的本性。
en: It is true that we cannot help disliking some people — this is human nature, after all.

zh: 很多时候，不查词典，你也能猜出生词的意思。
en: A lot of the time, you'll be able to guess the meaning of an unfamiliar word without looking it up.

zh: 你听得越多，越能听明白。
en: The more you listen, the easier it is to catch on.

zh: 除了发音，我们还用不同的词描述同样的事物。
en: Apart from pronunciation, we use different words for describing the same things.

zh: 而且，知道人的记忆如何随着年龄而改变，这真的很神奇。
en: What's more, it is amazing to know how a person's memory changes with age.

zh: 了解我们记忆的秘密意味着不仅要了解有关它的事实，还要了解提升它的方法。
en: Getting to know the secrets of our memory means learning not only the facts about it, but also the ways to improve it.

zh: 嘿，小吴，你喜欢艺术，对吧？
en: Hey Xiao Wu, you like art, don't you?

zh: 你喜欢哪些类型的艺术？
en: What types of art are you into?

zh: 嗯，大多数类型我都喜欢。
en: Well, I'm a fan of most types.

zh: 我真的很喜欢舞蹈和乐队表演，而且我经常看京剧。
en: I really enjoy dance and band performances and I often watch Beijing Opera.

zh: 我业余时间也画画，但我最喜欢的是古典音乐。
en: I also paint in my spare time, but my favourite thing is classical music.

zh: 你经常去听音乐会吗？
en: Do you often go to concerts?

zh: 是的，我尽量每月都去。
en: Yes. I try to go monthly.

zh: 你最喜欢它们哪些方面？
en: What do you enjoy most about them?

zh: 音乐家们太棒了。
en: The musicians are just amazing.

zh: 他们都很有才华且专业。
en: They are all very talented and professional.

zh: 最棒的部分是音乐厅的氛围以及所有不同类型乐器的完美结合。
en: And the best part is the atmosphere at the concert hall and the perfect combination of all the different types of instruments.

zh: 有几个小时，你可以放松一下，享受令人振奋的表演。
en: For a few hours, you can just relax and enjoy the uplifting performances.

zh: 我也真的很喜欢古典音乐，但我从未去过音乐会。
en: I really like classical music too, but I've never been to a concert.

zh: 下次我们可以一起去。
en: We could go together next time.

zh: 黛西，周六下午你要做什么？
en: What are you doing on Saturday afternoon, Daisy?

zh: 我有京剧的票！
en: I have tickets for Beijing Opera!

zh: 我认为这将是一场非常精彩的演出！
en: I think it's going to be a fantastic show!

zh: 谢谢你邀请我，埃里克，但我非常抱歉。
en: Thank you for inviting me, Eric, but I'm terribly sorry.

zh: 我要去一个现代艺术展。
en: I'm going to an exhibition of modern art.

zh: 我认为那会很棒。
en: I think it's going to be wonderful.

zh: 我真的很喜欢这次展览中的艺术家。
en: I really like the artists in this exhibition.

zh: 我通常看不懂那些画！
en: I usually don't understand the paintings!

zh: 嗯，每幅画都讲述一个故事，每个人可能从中看到不同的故事。
en: Well, each painting tells a story and each person may see a different story in it.

zh: 下次我应该和你一起去，你可以给我讲讲那些故事。
en: Next time I should go with you and you can tell me the stories.

zh: 希望你喜欢你的京剧。
en: Hope you enjoy your Beijing Opera.

zh: 我喜欢那些服装、舞台设计和充满活力的表演。
en: I love the costumes, the stage design and the energetic performance.

zh: 在今天的节目中，我们将与刘博士讨论京剧。
en: On today’s programme, we're going to discuss Beijing Opera with Dr. Liu.

zh: 刘博士，什么是京剧？它是什么时候开始的？
en: So, Dr. Liu, what is Beijing Opera and when did it start?

zh: 嗯，京剧是中国戏曲的一种形式，它将表演、念白、歌唱、音乐、舞蹈和杂技结合在一起，起源于十八世纪末。
en: Well, Beijing Opera is a form of Chinese opera which combines acting, talking, singing, music, dancing and acrobatics together and it started in the late eighteenth century.

zh: 好的。它运用了表演、念白、歌唱、音乐、舞蹈和杂技。
en: OK. It uses acting, talking, singing, music, dancing and acrobatics.

zh: 所有这些都很难掌握！
en: All of those are difficult to master!

zh: 是的。这就是为什么它是中国的国宝之一。
en: Yes. That's why it's one of China's national treasures.

zh: 确实！据说京剧表演者可以扮演四种角色。
en: Indeed! It is said that there are four roles Beijing Opera performers can play.

zh: 这四种角色是：生，即普通男性角色；旦，即女性角色；净，即画着鲜艳脸谱的男性角色；丑，即丑角。
en: The four roles are: sheng, which are common male roles; dan, which are female roles; jing, which are male roles with brightly painted masks; and chou, which are clown roles.

zh: 每个角色都有其独特的唱腔和表演风格。
en: Each role has its own singing and acting styles.

zh: 我明白了。我们知道京剧有一种非常独特的声音。
en: I see. We know Beijing Opera has a very unique sound.

zh: 你能告诉我们更多吗？
en: Can you tell us more?

zh: 当然。我想你指的是表演者经常用很高的嗓音演唱这一事实。
en: Sure. I think you're referring to the fact that performers often sing with very high voices.

zh: 这是因为在早期，京剧通常在露天舞台上表演。
en: This is because in the early days, Beijing Opera was usually performed on open-air stages.

zh: 为了让人群听到，音乐必须响亮，表演者必须用非常高的嗓音演唱。
en: In order to be heard over the crowds, the music had to be loud and performers had to sing in very high voices.

zh: 哇，我在这里肯定学到新东西了。
en: Wow, I'm definitely learning something new here.

zh: 我也对那些漂亮的服装感兴趣。
en: I'm also interested in the beautiful costumes.

zh: 哦，你知道它们为什么总是这么鲜艳的颜色吗？
en: Oh, do you know why they're always in such bright colours?

zh: 嗯，在过去，京剧经常在只用油灯照明的舞台上表演。
en: Well, in the past, Beijing Opera was often performed on stages that were lit only by oil lamps.

zh: 服装必须有明亮多彩的图案。
en: The costumes had to have bright and colourful patterns.

zh: 否则，就很难吸引观众的注意力。
en: Otherwise, it would have been difficult to attract the audience's attention.

zh: 那么，露西，怎么样？你喜欢你的第一次京剧体验吗？
en: So, Lucy, how was it? Did you enjoy your first Beijing Opera?

zh: 是的，我当然喜欢！这是一场精彩的演出……如此丰富多彩，激动人心！
en: Yes, I certainly did! It was an excellent show ... so colourful and exciting!

zh: 太好了！我很高兴你喜欢它。
en: Great! I'm so glad you enjoyed it.

zh: 我之前很担心，因为京剧和美国的话剧演出非常不同。
en: I was worried because Beijing Opera is so different from theatre shows in the US.

zh: 它是非常不同，但是是好的不同！
en: It was very different, but in a good way!

zh: 我喜欢里面的舞蹈和杂技。
en: I loved the dancing and the acrobatics.

zh: 表演者们太有才华了！
en: The performers were so talented!

zh: 但是有相当多的事情我不明白。
en: But there were quite a few things I didn't understand.

zh: 当然。也许我能帮忙。
en: Sure. Maybe I can help.

zh: 嗯，你知道主角两边各有一面旗子走过舞台的那部分吗？
en: Well, you know the part where the main character walks across the stage with a flag on each side of her?

zh: 我完全不知道她在做什么。
en: I had no idea what she was doing.

zh: 哦，那表示她正在乘坐马车。
en: Oh, that means she's riding in a carriage.

zh: 京剧中的简单动作常常代表更复杂的行动，比如骑马或划船。
en: Simple movements in Beijing Opera often represent more complex actions, like riding a horse or rowing a boat.

zh: 似乎京剧的很多方面都源于古老的传统。
en: It seems like so many aspects of Beijing Opera come from ancient traditions.

zh: 我真希望我对它们了解得更多。
en: I wish I knew more about them.

zh: 如果你感兴趣，我们可以一起去看更多的京剧。
en: If you're interested, we can go and watch more Beijing Opera together.

zh: 也许有一天你会成为专家！
en: Maybe one day you'll be an expert!

zh: 大家好，欢迎来到我们的绿色生活展览。
en: Dear all, welcome to our exhibition on green living.

zh: 你可能想知道我们所说的绿色生活是什么意思。
en: You may wonder what we mean by green living.

zh: 我相信在展览之后你会理解这个概念。
en: I'm sure you will understand this concept after the exhibition.

zh: 总共有四个部分。
en: There are four sections altogether.

zh: 第一部分介绍了不同类型的污染。
en: The first section introduces different types of pollution.

zh: 我们都知道我们周围有各种类型的污染，它们可能引起不同种类的环境问题，例如水污染、空气污染、海洋污染、土壤污染和噪音污染。
en: We all know that there are various types of pollution around us, which may cause different kinds of environmental problems, for instance, water pollution, air pollution, ocean pollution, soil pollution and noise pollution.

zh: 这一部分将展示污染如何影响我们的环境和健康。
en: This section will show how pollution affects our environment and our health.

zh: 第二部分解释了导致不同类型污染的原因。
en: The second section explains the reasons that cause different types of pollution.

zh: 并且你将看到我们人类的行为是污染的主要来源。
en: And you will see that our actions as humans are the main sources of pollution.

zh: 第三部分介绍了为解决这些问题所采取的解决方案，详细说明了我们可以帮助环境的方法。
en: The third section presents the solutions that have been taken to tackle the problems, detailing ways we can help the environment.

zh: 例如，减少污染的一个好方法是种植更多的树木。
en: For example, a good way to reduce pollution is to plant more trees.

zh: 最后，展览以一份关于我们个人认为我们应该做什么来引领绿色生活的总结结束。
en: Finally, the exhibition ends with a summary about what we personally believe we should be doing to lead a green life.

zh: 对我来说，绿色生活就是与自然和谐相处。
en: To me, green living is about living in harmony with nature.

zh: 我不是很喜欢住在城市的公寓里。
en: I don't really like living in a flat in the city.

zh: 我更喜欢有一个花园，因为我喜欢亲近自然。
en: I prefer to have a garden because I like being close to nature.

zh: 这是我花园的一张照片。
en: Here's a photo of my garden.

zh: 实际上，我住在一个公寓里，但我可以通过创造更多的绿色空间来帮助环境。
en: Actually, I live in a flat, but I can help the environment by creating more green spaces.

zh: 例如，我在窗外种了一些花和蔬菜，我也有很棒的视野。
en: For example, I plant some flowers and vegetables outside my window and I have a great view, too.

zh: 拥有更多的绿色空间有助于环境。
en: Having more green spaces helps the environment.

zh: 绿色空间在我们对抗像全球变暖这样的问题中非常重要。
en: Green spaces are very important in our fight against problems like global warming.

zh: 今天，我要谈谈NPO-Greenlife。
en: Today, I'm going to talk about NPO-Greenlife.

zh: 易解放女士是慈善组织NPO-Greenlife的创始人。
en: Ms Yi Jiefang is the founder of NPO-Greenlife, a charity organisation.

zh: 她为什么建立这样一个组织，它是为了什么？
en: Why did she set up such an organisation and what is it for?

zh: 这些就是我在我的演讲中要谈论的事情。
en: These are the things I'm going to talk about in my presentation.

zh: 易女士曾经有一个满足的生活，直到一个可怕的事件发生，改变了她的生活。
en: Ms Yi had a contented life until a terrible event occurred which changed her life.

zh: 她22岁的儿子在一次道路事故中去世了。
en: Her 22-year-old son died in a road accident.

zh: 对易解放来说，生活似乎失去了所有的意义。
en: For Yi Jiefang, life seemed to lose all meaning.

zh: 但尽管悲伤，她记得她的儿子曾经多么关心中国北方的沙尘暴和沙漠。
en: But despite her sadness, she remembered how concerned her son had been about the sandstorms and deserts in northern China.

zh: 他说过他毕业后想去那里种树。
en: He had said that he wanted to plant trees there after he graduated.

zh: 所以易解放决定让她儿子的梦想成真，NPO-Greenlife就此诞生了。
en: So Yi Jiefang decided to make her son's dream come true, and NPO-Greenlife was born.

zh: NPO-Greenlife的早期日子并不容易。
en: The early days of NPO-Greenlife were not easy.

zh: 易解放去了内蒙古的沙漠，看到了大片的干旱土地，几乎没有农业。
en: Yi Jiefang went to the deserts of Inner Mongolia and saw vast areas of dry lands with little agriculture.

zh: 同意了她关于十年内在该地区种植一百万棵树的提议。
en: agreed with her proposal to plant one million trees in the region over ten years.

zh: 然而，她的团队几乎没有植树经验，第一批树被吹走了！
en: However, her team had little experience of tree planting and the first trees were blown away!

zh: 尽管树木存活的机会似乎很渺茫，但困难并没有阻止她尝试。
en: Although there seemed only a slim chance that the trees could survive, the difficulty did not prevent her from trying.

zh: 永不放弃一直是她的原则。
en: It had always been her policy never to give up.

zh: 现在树木的存活率为85%。
en: Now the trees have a survival rate of 85%.

zh: 我想与大家分享写在易解放儿子墓碑上的几句话：
en: I'd like to share with you a couple of quotes that are written on the headstone of Yi Jiefang's son:

zh: “你是一棵树。无论活着还是倒下，你都实现了价值。
en: “You are a tree. Alive or fallen, you served a purpose.

zh: 活着，你傲然挺立，抵御风沙；倒下，你的光芒给予他人光明和温暖。”
en: Alive, you stood proud against winds and sandstorms; fallen, your brightness gave light and warmth to others.”

zh: “让生命在绿色中繁荣；让绿色在生命中永存。”
en: “Let life prosper in greenness; let greenness last forever in life.”

zh: 我认为从易解放的故事中可以学到很多。
en: I think a lot can be learnt from the story of Yi Jiefang.

zh: 我完全尊重她，并钦佩她克服如此巨大个人损失的力量。
en: I have complete respect for her and admire her strength in overcoming such a great personal loss.

zh: 她决心让儿子的梦想成为现实，从而带来了巨大的改变。
en: She has made a huge difference with her determination to make her son's dreams a reality.

zh: 我的演讲到此结束。
en: That brings my presentation to an end.

zh: 陈博士，谢谢您的演讲。
en: Dr. Chen, thanks for your presentation.

zh: 我学到了很多关于易解放女士的事。
en: I learnt a lot about Ms Yi Jiefang.

zh: 我的问题是：易解放女士是如何在她儿子去世后走出悲伤的？
en: My question is: How did Ms Yi Jiefang work through her sadness after her son's death?

zh: 嗯，正如你们所听到的，这真是一个关于母子之爱的故事。
en: Well, as you've heard, this is really a story of the love between mother and son.

zh: 她儿子的愿望给了易重建生活的力量。
en: Her son's wishes gave Yi the strength to rebuild her life.

zh: “让沙漠变绿是我儿子的梦想。
en: “To make the desert green was my son's dream.

zh: 让他的梦想成真就是我生命的意义，”她说。
en: To make his dream come true is the meaning of my life,” she has said.

zh: 您能谈谈沙漠过去和现在的状况吗？
en: Can you say something about the former and the current conditions in the desert?

zh: 易选择的沙漠被称为“死亡之海”。
en: The desert Yi chose was referred to as “the Sea of Death”.

zh: 但在NPO绿色生命组织的努力下，那里发生了巨大的变化。
en: But with the efforts made by NPO-Greenlife, huge changes have been seen there.

zh: 草地已逐渐开始从沙土中生长出来。
en: Grassland has gradually started to grow from the sandy soil.

zh: 村民们现在甚至可以种西瓜了。
en: Villagers can even grow watermelons now.

zh: 当地曾经干涸的河流现在水流不断，并且有很多鱼可以捕捞。
en: And the local river, which used to be dry, now runs and is full of fish to catch.

zh: 陈博士，我对她现在正在做的事情很感兴趣。
en: Dr. Chen, I'm interested in what she is working on now.

zh: 您能告诉我们更多吗？
en: Could you tell us more?

zh: 2013年，易开始了一个新项目，在内蒙古种植更多的树。
en: In 2013, Yi began a new project to plant more trees in Inner Mongolia.

zh: 这次她选择了影响北京的许多沙尘暴起源的那个县。
en: This time she chose the county where many of the sandstorms that affect Beijing start.

zh: 她是我们所有人的榜样。
en: She's an inspiration to all of us.

zh: 易已经表明，所谓的“普通人”也能做出惊人的事情。
en: Yi has shown that so-called “ordinary people” can do amazing things.

zh: 现在很多人自愿在她的组织中工作。
en: Many people now volunteer to work in her organisation.

zh: 我们真的可以一起取得如此多的成就。
en: We really can achieve so much together.

zh: 我最喜欢的科目是物理。
en: My favourite subject is physics.

zh: 我喜欢学习新思想。
en: I like to learn about new ideas.

zh: 总是对新事物充满好奇。
en: always curious about new things.

zh: 太好了。你正在和朋友们一起做一个物理项目，对吗？
en: Great. You're working on a physics project with your friends, right?

zh: 是的，我是。这很棒。我喜欢课后与伙伴合作或进行小组工作。
en: Yes, I am. It's great. I like to work with a partner or do group work after class.

zh: 你的学习目标是什么？
en: What is your learning goal?

zh: 我的学习目标是考上大学。而且我下个月要参加县里的科学竞赛。
en: My learning goal is to get into college. And I'm going to enter the county science competition next month.

zh: 祝你好运！我相信你会做得很好的。
en: Good luck! I'm sure you'll do well.

zh: 嗨，你好。你要去哪里？
en: Hi, there. Where are you off to?

zh: 我要去图书馆。我正在为下周的英语考试复习。
en: I'm going to the library. I'm revising for an English exam next week.

zh: 你一直这么努力。应该不会有任何问题的！
en: You've been working so hard. There shouldn't be any problem!

zh: 但我还是需要温习一些语言点。
en: But I still need to brush up on some language points.

zh: 加油，你会没事的。你经常用你惊人的记忆力让我们感到惊讶。
en: Come on, you'll be fine. You often surprise us with your amazing memory.

zh: 但过去这几天我睡眠不好，而且我更喜欢在安静的地方独自学习，这样我可以集中注意力，把细节清晰地记在脑子里。
en: But I've been sleeping badly these past few days, and I just prefer to study alone in a quiet place so that I can concentrate and keep the details straight in my head.

zh: 欢迎来到今天的节目，我们邀请了史密斯博士与我们分享学习英语的有效策略。
en: Welcome to today's programme, We've invited Dr. Smith to share with us effective strategies for learning English.

zh: 你好，史密斯博士，我们的许多听众都有关于学习英语的问题。
en: Hello, Dr. Smith, many of our listeners have questions,about learning English.

zh: 我想你已经准备好接听我们听众的电话了。
en: I suppose you're ready to take calls from our listeners.

zh: 你好，史密斯博士。我花了很多时间阅读英语课本，但进步甚微。
en: Hello, Dr. Smith. I've spent a lot of time reading English coursebooks, but I’ve made little progress.

zh: 我的问题是如何有效地学习英语。
en: My question is how to learn English effectively.

zh: 嗯，我的建议是在课堂之外增加你的英语知识。
en: Well, my advice is to increase your knowledge of English beyond the classroom.

zh: 一种方法是多阅读。
en: One way to do that is by reading more.

zh: 我建议你尝试简化的经典文学作品，比如用简单英语重写的短篇小说和长篇小说。
en: I would recommend that you try simplified classic literature, such as short stories and novels rewritten in simple English.

zh: 另一种方法是多听，例如，听英语节目。
en: The other way is to listen more, for example, listening to English programmes.

zh: 有了足够的语言输入，我相信你能有效地学习英语。
en: With sufficient language input, I'm sure you can learn English effectively.

zh: 你好，史密斯博士。我叫杨安。我发现记新单词非常困难。
en: Hello, Dr. Smith. My name is Yang An. I find memorising new words very difficult.

zh: 嗨，杨安。那你通常是怎么记新单词的？
en: Hi, Yang An. So what do you normally do to memorise new words?

zh: 我只是试着背单词表，这非常困难和无聊。
en: I just try to memorise the word list and it is so difficult and boring.

zh: 嗯，我认为背单词表不是学习单词的最佳方式。
en: Well, I don't think memorising the word list is the best way to learn words.

zh: 首先，你应该在语境中学习单词。
en: First, you should learn words in context.

zh: 例如，在阅读英语故事时，即使遇到一些新单词，也要试着继续读下去。
en: For example, when reading English stories, try to keep reading on even when you come across some new words.

zh: 很多时候，你能够在不查字典的情况下猜出一个不熟悉单词的意思。
en: A lot of the time, you'll be able to guess the meaning of an unfamiliar word without looking it up.

zh: 此外，你应该以词块的形式学习单词。
en: Also, you should learn words in chunks.

zh: 例如，我们说“open the book”，但说“turn on the computer”。
en: For example, we say "open the book", but "turn on the computer".

zh: 或者我们说“eat an apple”，但说“take the medicine”。
en: Or we say "eat an apple", but "take the medicine".

zh: 好的。我明白了。所以我需要在语境中和以词块的形式学习单词。
en: OK. I see. So I need to learn words in context and in chunks.

zh: S: 你好，史密斯博士。我是苏琴。学习语法对我来说是个大难题。我花了大量时间记忆语法规则和做大量练习，但我的英语仍然很差。
en: S: Hello, Dr. Smith. I'm Su Qin. Learning grammar is a big headache for me. I've put so much time in memorising grammar rules and doing a lot of exercises, but my English is still really bad.

zh: DS: 嗨，苏琴。仅仅记忆规则是没有帮助的。我的建议是，当你学习一个语法规则时，你应该尝试在口语和写作活动中使用它。通过这样做，你将以一种比语法书更自然的方式学习语法。慢慢地，你会开始掌握这些规则并在不加思考的情况下使用它们。
en: DS: Hi, Su Qin. Just memorising the rules is not helpful. My advice is that when you learn a grammar rule, you should try to use it in speaking and writing activities. By doing so, you'll be learning grammar in a more natural way than from a grammar book. Very slowly, you will start to acquire the rules and use them without thinking.

zh: S: 啊！我会试试看效果如何。非常感谢您。
en: S: Ah! I'll try this and see how it goes. Thank you very much.

zh: I: 谢谢您，史密斯博士，今天加入我们。我相信我们的观众现在对如何有效学习英语有了更好的想法。
en: I: Thank you, Dr. Smith, for joining us today. I'm sure our audience has better ideas about how to learn English effectively now.

zh: L: 英语在世界上许多地区被使用，例如美国、英国、澳大利亚和新西兰。因此，英语的使用可能因国家而异。不同地区的英语使用者也可能有不同的说话习惯。今天，我们很荣幸有两位特别嘉宾来告诉我们这一点。
en: L: English is spoken in many areas of the world, for example, the US, the UK, Australia and New Zealand. Therefore, the English language may be used slightly differently from one country to another. English speakers in different areas may also have different speaking habits. Today, we're honoured to have two special guests with us to tell us about this.

zh: P: 嘿！我是帕姆，来自美国。
en: P: Hey there! I'm Pam, from the US.

zh: R: 你好，很高兴认识你。我是罗伯特，来自英国。
en: R: Hello, nice to meet you. I'm Robert, from the UK.

zh: L: 很高兴认识你们，帕姆和罗伯特。你们能告诉我们美式英语和英式英语的区别吗？
en: L: Nice to meet you, Pam and Robert. Can you tell us the differences between American English and British English?

zh: P: 嗯，我是美国人，罗伯特是英国人，所以我们有不同的单词发音方式。例如；英国人说“water”和“tomato”，但美国人说“water”和“tomato”！
en: P: Well, I'm American and Robert is British, so we have different ways of pronouncing words. For example; British people say “water” and "tomato" but Americans say “water” and "tomato"!

zh: L: 这真奇怪！它们听起来如此不同！它们的意思还是一样的吗？
en: L: That's odd! They sound so different! Do they still mean the same thing?

zh: P: 是一样的。让我为你想一些其他的例子。啊，我说“example”和“dancing in the classroom”，但罗伯特说……
en: P: They do. Let me think of some other examples for you. Ah, I say “example” and “dancing in the classroom", but Robert says ...

zh: R: “Example”和“dancing in the classroom”。
en: R: "Example" and “dancing in the classroom”.

zh: L: 啊！当单词的发音如此不同时，人们如何相互理解？
en: L: Ah! How do people understand each other when words are pronounced so differently?

zh: P: 你听得越多，就越容易理解。
en: P: The more you listen, the easier it is to catch on.

zh: R: 除了发音，我们用不同的词来描述相同的事物。例如，我们说“lift”，但美国人说“elevator”。我们说“football”，美国人说“soccer”！
en: R: Apart from pronunciation, we use different words for describing the same things. For example, we say “lift” but Americans say “elevator”. We say football" and Americans say “soccer”!

zh: R: 让我告诉你一个笑话。在伦敦，如果你问当地人，“最近的地铁在哪里？”，你最终可能会去一家快餐店！美国的“subway”在英国被称为“underground”，或者在伦敦被称为“tube”。
en: R: Let me tell you a joke. In London, if you ask the local people, “Where is the nearest subway?”, you may end up in a fast food restaurant! The American “subway” is called the “underground” in the UK, or the “tube” in London.

zh: L: 哇！我从未意识到英式英语和美式英语如此不同！
en: L: Wow! I never realised that British and American English were so different!

zh: P: 嗯，别担心。尽管它们在某种程度上有所不同，但人们相互理解没有困难。还有其他沟通方式——微笑是一种世界性的语言！
en: P: Well, don’t worry. Though they are different in one way or another, people have no difficulty in understanding each other. There are other ways to communicate — smiling is a worldwide language!
